Por el contrario, si la lente se adelgaza tirando de ella, el objeto aparece pequeño y borroso. Las gotas de agua son similares a los ojos humanos, pero cuando tocan la superficie de un objeto, se juntan en un círculo debido a su tendencia a aglomerarse. Si observa detenidamente la parte redonda, la superficie parece agrandada. Al igual que un músculo empuja y tira de la lente, las gotas de agua aumentan y disminuyen la cantidad de agua para enfocar.
La función de "empujar y tirar automático" de la cámara utiliza el principio de esta gota de agua. En cámaras anteriores, para enfocar, se apilaban varios lentes y la distancia entre los lentes tenía que cambiarse con un dispositivo mecánico. Así que el tamaño de la cámara era grande y pesado. Sin embargo, en estos días, el principio de las gotas de agua se introduce en la cámara, lo que permite tomar fotos de cerca y de larga distancia con un teléfono móvil pequeño.

g_text_translate.id:
Ketika seseorang melihat suatu objek, itu karena mata memiliki 'lensa kristal'. Jika otot mendorong 'lensa kristal' untuk membuatnya lebih tebal, objek tampak lebih besar dan lebih akurat. Sebaliknya, jika lensa dibuat lebih tipis dengan menariknya, objek tampak kecil dan buram. Tetesan air mirip dengan mata manusia, tetapi ketika mereka menyentuh permukaan suatu benda, mereka berkumpul dalam lingkaran karena kecenderungan mereka untuk menggumpal. Jika diperhatikan dengan seksama bagian yang bulat, permukaannya tampak membesar. Sama seperti otot yang mendorong dan menarik lensa, tetesan air menambah dan mengurangi jumlah air untuk difokuskan.
Fungsi 'dorong dan tarik otomatis' kamera menggunakan prinsip tetesan air ini. Pada kamera sebelumnya, untuk fokus, beberapa lensa ditumpuk dan jarak antara lensa harus diubah dengan perangkat mekanis. Jadi ukuran kameranya besar dan berat. Namun, belakangan ini, prinsip tetesan air diperkenalkan ke dalam kamera, sehingga memungkinkan untuk mengambil foto jarak dekat dan jarak jauh dengan ponsel kecil.

g_text_translate.ja:
人が物を見るとき、それは目に「水晶体」があるからです。筋肉が「水晶体」を押して厚くすると、物体はより大きく正確に見えます。逆にレンズを引っ張って薄くすると、物が小さくぼやけて見えます。水滴は人間の目に似ていますが、物体の表面に触れると、凝集する傾向があるため、円形に集まります。丸い部分をよく見ると、表面が拡大して見えます。筋肉がレンズを押したり引いたりするのと同じように、水滴は焦点を合わせる水の量を増減します。
カメラの「自動押し引き」機能は、この水滴の原理を利用しています。従来のカメラでは、ピントを合わせるために複数のレンズを重ね、レンズ間の距離を機械装置で変更する必要がありました。そのため、カメラのサイズは大きくて重いものでした。しかし、最近では水滴の原理がカメラに取り入れられ、小さな携帯電話でも近距離や遠距離の写真が撮れるようになりました。

g_text_translate.de:
Wenn eine Person ein Objekt sieht, liegt das daran, dass das Auge eine „Kristalllinse“ hat. Wenn der Muskel die „Kristalllinse“ drückt, um sie dicker zu machen, erscheinen Objekte größer und genauer. Wird die Linse hingegen durch Ziehen dünner gemacht, erscheint das Objekt klein und verschwommen. Wassertropfen ähneln menschlichen Augen, aber wenn sie die Oberfläche eines Objekts berühren, sammeln sie sich aufgrund ihrer Neigung zur Agglomeration in einem Kreis. Betrachtet man den runden Teil genau, erscheint die Oberfläche vergrößert. So wie ein Muskel die Linse drückt und zieht, erhöhen und verringern Wassertropfen die zu fokussierende Wassermenge.
Die „automatische Push-and-Pull“-Funktion der Kamera nutzt das Prinzip dieses Wassertropfens. Bei früheren Kameras wurden zum Fokussieren mehrere Linsen gestapelt und der Abstand zwischen den Linsen musste mit einer mechanischen Vorrichtung geändert werden. Die Größe der Kamera war also groß und schwer. Heutzutage wird jedoch das Prinzip der Wassertropfen in die Kamera eingeführt, wodurch es möglich ist, mit einem kleinen Mobiltelefon Nahaufnahmen und Fernaufnahmen zu machen.

#######

The ‘automatic push and pull’ function of the camera uses the principle of this water droplet. In previous cameras, in order to focus, multiple lenses were stacked and the distance between the lenses had to be changed with a mechanical device. So the size of the camera was large and heavy. However, these days, the principle of water droplets is introduced into the camera, making it possible to take close-up and long-distance photos with a small mobile phone.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
当一个人看到一个物体时，那是因为眼睛有一个“晶状体”。如果肌肉推动“晶状体”使其变厚，物体就会显得更大、更准确。相反，如果通过拉动镜片使其变薄，则物体会显得小而模糊。水滴类似于人的眼睛，但是当它们接触到物体表面时，由于它们容易结块，它们会聚集成一个圆圈。如果仔细观察圆形部分，表面会显得放大。就像肌肉推拉镜头一样，水滴会增加和减少聚焦的水量。
相机的“自动推拉”功能就是利用这种水滴的原理。在以前的相机中，为了对焦，需要堆叠多个镜头，并且必须通过机械装置改变镜头之间的距离。所以相机的尺寸又大又重。然而，如今，水滴的原理被引入到相机中，使得用小手机也能拍出近景和远距离的照片。

g_text_translate.it:
Quando una persona vede un oggetto, è perché l'occhio ha una "lente cristallina". Se il muscolo spinge la "lente cristallina" per renderla più spessa, gli oggetti appaiono più grandi e più precisi. Al contrario, se la lente viene assottigliata tirandola, l'oggetto appare piccolo e sfocato. Le goccioline d'acqua sono simili agli occhi umani, ma quando toccano la superficie di un oggetto, si raccolgono in cerchio per la loro tendenza ad agglomerarsi. La funzione "push and pull automatico" della fotocamera utilizza il principio di questa goccia d'acqua. Quindi le dimensioni della fotocamera erano grandi e pesanti. Tuttavia, oggigiorno, nella fotocamera viene introdotto il principio delle gocce d'acqua, che consente di scattare foto ravvicinate e a lunga distanza con un piccolo telefono cellulare.

g_text_translate.ru:
Когда человек видит объект, это происходит потому, что в глазу есть «хрусталик». Если мышца толкает «хрусталик», чтобы сделать его толще, объекты кажутся больше и точнее. И наоборот, если линзу сделать тоньше, потянув ее, объект будет казаться маленьким и размытым. Капли воды похожи на глаза человека, но когда они касаются поверхности объекта, они собираются в круг из-за склонности к агломерации. Если внимательно присмотреться к круглой части, поверхность покажется увеличенной. Точно так же, как мышца толкает и тянет хрусталик, капли воды увеличивают и уменьшают количество воды, необходимое для фокусировки.
Функция «автоматического нажатия и вытягивания» камеры использует принцип этой капли воды. В предыдущих камерах для фокусировки приходилось складывать несколько линз, и расстояние между линзами приходилось изменять с помощью механического устройства. Поэтому размер камеры был большим и тяжелым. Однако в наши дни в камеру внедрен принцип капель воды, что позволяет делать фотографии крупным планом и на большом расстоянии с помощью небольшого мобильного телефона.

g_text_translate.pt:
Quando uma pessoa vê um objeto é porque o olho possui uma “lente cristalina”. Se o músculo empurra a “lente cristalina” para torná-la mais espessa, os objetos parecem maiores e mais precisos. Por outro lado, se a lente ficar mais fina ao puxá-la, o objeto parecerá pequeno e embaçado. As gotas de água são semelhantes aos olhos humanos, mas quando tocam a superfície de um objeto, formam um círculo devido à sua tendência à aglomeração. Se você olhar atentamente para a parte redonda, a superfície parecerá ampliada. Assim como um músculo empurra e puxa a lente, as gotas de água aumentam e diminuem a quantidade de água para focar.
A função ‘empurrar e puxar automaticamente’ da câmera usa o princípio desta gota de água. Nas câmeras anteriores, para focar, múltiplas lentes eram empilhadas e a distância entre as lentes tinha que ser alterada com um dispositivo mecânico. Portanto, o tamanho da câmera era grande e pesado. Porém, hoje em dia, o princípio das gotas de água é introduzido na câmera, possibilitando tirar fotos de perto e de longa distância com um pequeno celular.

However, these days, the principle of water droplets is introduced into the camera, making it possible to take close-up and long-distance photos with a small mobile phone.
g_text_translate.hi:
जब कोई व्यक्ति किसी वस्तु को देखता है, तो ऐसा इसलिए होता है क्योंकि आंख में 'क्रिस्टलीय लेंस' होता है। यदि मांसपेशी 'क्रिस्टलीय लेंस' को मोटा करने के लिए उसे धक्का देती है, तो वस्तुएं बड़ी और अधिक सटीक दिखाई देती हैं। इसके विपरीत यदि लेंस को खींचकर पतला कर दिया जाए तो वस्तु छोटी और धुंधली दिखाई देती है। पानी की बूंदें इंसान की आंखों के समान होती हैं, लेकिन जब वे किसी वस्तु की सतह को छूती हैं, तो एकत्रित होने की प्रवृत्ति के कारण एक घेरे में इकट्ठा हो जाती हैं। यदि आप गोल भाग को ध्यान से देखें तो सतह बढ़ी हुई दिखाई देती है। जैसे एक मांसपेशी लेंस को धक्का देती है और खींचती है, वैसे ही पानी की बूंदें फोकस करने के लिए पानी की मात्रा को बढ़ाती और घटाती हैं।
कैमरे का 'स्वचालित पुश और पुल' फ़ंक्शन इस पानी की बूंद के सिद्धांत का उपयोग करता है। पिछले कैमरों में, फोकस करने के लिए, कई लेंसों को एक साथ रखा जाता था और लेंसों के बीच की दूरी को एक यांत्रिक उपकरण से बदलना पड़ता था। इसलिए कैमरे का आकार बड़ा और भारी था। हालाँकि, इन दिनों, पानी की बूंदों के सिद्धांत को कैमरे में पेश किया गया है, जिससे छोटे मोबाइल फोन से क्लोज़-अप और लंबी दूरी की तस्वीरें लेना संभव हो गया है।

#######

When a person sees an object, it is because the eye has a ‘crystalline lens’. If the muscle pushes the ‘crystalline lens’ to make it thicker, objects appear larger and more accurate. Conversely, if the lens is made thinner by pulling it, the object appears small and blurry. Water droplets are similar to human eyes, but when they touch the surface of an object, they gather in a circle due to their tendency to agglomerate. If you look closely at the round part, the surface appears enlarged. Just like a muscle pushes and pulls the lens, water droplets increase and decrease the amount of water to focus.
The ‘automatic push and pull’ function of the camera uses the principle of this water droplet. In previous cameras, in order to focus, multiple lenses were stacked and the distance between the lenses had to be changed with a mechanical device. So the size of the camera was large and heavy. However, these days, the principle of water droplets is introduced into the camera, making it possible to take close-up and long-distance photos with a small mobile phone.
g_text_translate.tr:
Bir kişi bir nesneyi gördüğünde bunun nedeni, gözün 'kristalin bir merceğe' sahip olmasıdır. Kas, 'kristal merceği' kalınlaştırmak için iterse nesneler daha büyük ve daha doğru görünür. Tersine, mercek çekilerek inceltilirse nesne küçük ve bulanık görünür. Su damlacıkları insan gözüne benzer ancak bir nesnenin yüzeyine temas ettiğinde topaklanma eğiliminden dolayı bir daire şeklinde toplanırlar. Yuvarlak kısma yakından baktığınızda yüzey büyümüş görünüyor. Tıpkı bir kasın merceği itip çekmesi gibi, su damlacıkları da odaklanacak su miktarını artırır ve azaltır.
Kameranın 'otomatik itme ve çekme' işlevi bu su damlacıklarının prensibini kullanır. Önceki kameralarda odaklanabilmek için birden fazla mercek üst üste konuluyordu ve mercekler arasındaki mesafenin mekanik bir cihazla değiştirilmesi gerekiyordu. Yani kameranın boyutu büyük ve ağırdı. Ancak günümüzde kameralara su damlacıkları prensibi getirilerek küçük bir cep telefonuyla yakın ve uzak mesafeli fotoğraflar çekilmesi mümkün hale geldi.

#######

When a person sees an object, it is because the eye has a ‘crystalline lens’. If the muscle pushes the ‘crystalline lens’ to make it thicker, objects appear larger and more accurate. Conversely, if the lens is made thinner by pulling it, the object appears small and blurry. Water droplets are similar to human eyes, but when they touch the surface of an object, they gather in a circle due to their tendency to agglomerate. If you look closely at the round part, the surface appears enlarged. Just like a muscle pushes and pulls the lens, water droplets increase and decrease the amount of water to focus.
The ‘automatic push and pull’ function of the camera uses the principle of this water droplet. In previous cameras, in order to focus, multiple lenses were stacked and the distance between the lenses had to be changed with a mechanical device. So the size of the camera was large and heavy. However, these days, the principle of water droplets is introduced into the camera, making it possible to take close-up and long-distance photos with a small mobile phone.

content.q_text: 이 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 근육이 수정체를 밀면 수정체가 오목해진다.
예전의 사진기에는 ‘밀고 당기기’ 기능이 없었다.
수정체는 두께를 조절하여 사물의 초점을 맞춘다.
물방울과 수정체는 동그랗게 모이려는 성질이 있다.

explain.vi:
Chọn câu giống với nội dung của đoạn văn.
1. Nếu cơ đẩy thủy tinh thể, thủy tinh thể sẽ bị lõm vào. 
2. Máy ảnh trước đây không có chức năng 'kéo và đẩy'
3. Thủy tinh thể điều chỉnh độ dày để lấy nét của vật thể. 
4. Các giọt nước và thủy tinh thể có tính chất tập trung thành vòng tròn.
--------------------
Đoạn văn có câu "근육이... 맞출 수 있다. (Giống như cơ... lấy nét.)" Như vậy có nghĩa là thủy tinh thể điều chỉnh độ dày để lấy nét của vật thể. 3 là đáp án đúng.

4. Water droplets and the lens have a tendency to gather in a circle.
--------------------
<g>근육이 수정체를 밀고 당기는 것처럼 물방울은 물의 양을 늘리고 줄여서 초점을 맞출 수 있다:</g> Just like a muscle pushes and pulls the lens, water droplets increase and decrease the amount of water to focus.

This means that the lens adjusts its thickness to focus on the subject. 3 is the correct answer.

--------------------
<g>근육이 수정체를 밀고 당기는 것처럼 물방울은 물의 양을 늘리고 줄여서 초점을 맞출 수 있다:</g>Tout comme un muscle pousse et tire la lentille, les gouttelettes d'eau augmentent et diminuent la quantité d'eau à focaliser.

Cela signifie que l'objectif ajuste son épaisseur pour faire la mise au point sur le sujet. 3 est la bonne réponse.

explain.hi:

इसका मतलब है कि लेंस विषय पर ध्यान केंद्रित करने के लिए अपनी मोटाई समायोजित करता है। 3 सही उत्तर है.

general.g_text: 이처럼 과학적 예측이 풀리지 않는 수수께끼로 남는 경우는 많다. 그러나 최근에 37년 전의 과학적 예측이 실제 관측을 통해 증명된 바 있다. 지구에서 3만 광년 떨어진 곳에는 태양의 460만 배나 되는 거대 질량 블랙홀이 있는데, 천문학자들은 이 블랙홀이 다른 별을 삼키면 광선 다발이 발생해 갑자기 밝아지는 현상이 나타날 것이라고 예견한 바 있다. 그런데 최근 이 예견이 6개국 공동 연구팀에 의해 사실로 밝혀지게 되었다. 실제 관측된 바에 의하면 어떤 별이 블랙홀에 다가가면 매우 강한 중력의 작용으로 별이 산산이 깨져 버린다. 이 별의 부서진 조각들이 블랙홀에 빨려 들어갈 때 광선 다발이 특정한 방향으로 향하게 되는데 이때 강력한 빛이 발생하는 것이다. 만약 이 광선 다발이 (        ) 매우 위험하다. 그러나 이런 일은 1000억 년에 한 번 있을까 말까 할 정도로 발생 가능성이 매우 낮다.

g_text_translate.vi: Có nhiều trường hợp những dự đoán mang tính khoa học vẫn là bí ẩn chưa được giải đáp như thế. Tuy nhiên, mới đây, dự đoán mang tính khoa học của 37 năm trước đã được kiểm chứng qua những quan sát thực tế. Ở nơi có khoảng cách 30.000 năm ánh sáng từ Trái đất, có một lỗ đen khổng lồ với khối lượng gấp 4,6 triệu lần Mặt trời, các nhà thiên văn học dự đoán rằng khi lỗ đen này nuốt chửng một ngôi sao khác, nó sẽ tạo ra một bó tia sáng và xuất hiện hiện tượng đột ngột sáng lên. Nhưng gần đây, dự đoán này được phát hiện là sự thật bởi một nhóm nghiên cứu chung từ 6 quốc gia. Dựa trên quan sát thực tế, khi một ngôi sao nào đó đến gần lỗ đen, ngôi sao sẽ bị vỡ vụn do tác dụng của trọng lực rất mạnh. Khi các mảnh vỡ vụn của ngôi sao này bị hút vào lỗ đen, một bó tia sáng được định hướng theo một hướng cụ thể, khi đó tạo ra một luồng sáng mạnh. Sẽ rất nguy hiểm nếu bó tia sáng này (        ). Tuy nhiên, xác suất của một sự kiện như vậy xảy ra là rất thấp, nó chỉ xảy ra một lần trong 100 tỷ năm.
g_text_translate.en: In many cases, scientific predictions like this remain an unsolved mystery. However, recently, scientific predictions of 37 years ago have been verified through actual observations. At a distance of 30,000 light-years from Earth, there is a massive black hole with a mass of 4.6 million times that of the Sun, astronomers have predicted that if this black hole swallows another star, it will produce a bundle of rays and suddenly brighten. Recently, however, this prediction has been turned out to be true by a joint research team from six countries. According to actual observation, when a star approaches a black hole, the star is shattered by the action of a very strong gravitational force. As the shattered pieces of the star are sucked into the black hole, a bundle of rays is directed in a specific direction, generating a powerful light. It is very dangerous if this bundle of rays is emitted towards the Earth. However, the probability of such an event occurring is very low, such that it only happens once in 100 billion years.
g_text_translate.fr:
Dans de nombreux cas, des prédictions scientifiques comme celle-ci restent un mystère non résolu. Cependant, récemment, les prédictions scientifiques d'il y a 37 ans ont été vérifiées par des observations réelles. À une distance de 30 000 années-lumière de la Terre, il y a un trou noir massif d'une masse de 4,6 millions de fois celle du Soleil, les astronomes ont prédit que si ce trou noir avale une autre étoile, il produira un faisceau de rayons et soudainement éclairer. Récemment, cependant, cette prédiction s'est avérée vraie par une équipe de recherche conjointe de six pays. Selon l'observation réelle, lorsqu'une étoile s'approche d'un trou noir, l'étoile est brisée par l'action d'une force gravitationnelle très forte. Lorsque les morceaux brisés de l'étoile sont aspirés dans le trou noir, un faisceau de rayons est dirigé dans une direction spécifique, générant une lumière puissante. C'est très dangereux si ce faisceau de rayons est émis vers la Terre. Cependant, la probabilité qu'un tel événement se produise est très faible, de sorte qu'il ne se produit qu'une fois tous les 100 milliards d'années.

#######

In many cases, scientific predictions like this remain an unsolved mystery. However, recently, scientific predictions of 37 years ago have been verified through actual observations. At a distance of 30,000 light-years from Earth, there is a massive black hole with a mass of 4.6 million times that of the Sun, astronomers have predicted that if this black hole swallows another star, it will produce a bundle of rays and suddenly brighten. Recently, however, this prediction has been turned out to be true by a joint research team from six countries. According to actual observation, when a star approaches a black hole, the star is shattered by the action of a very strong gravitational force. As the shattered pieces of the star are sucked into the black hole, a bundle of rays is directed in a specific direction, generating a powerful light. It is very dangerous if this bundle of rays is emitted towards the Earth. However, the probability of such an event occurring is very low, such that it only happens once in 100 billion years.
g_text_translate.es:
En muchos casos, las predicciones científicas como esta siguen siendo un misterio sin resolver. Sin embargo, recientemente, las predicciones científicas de hace 37 años han sido verificadas a través de observaciones reales. A una distancia de 30.000 años luz de la Tierra, hay un agujero negro masivo con una masa de 4,6 millones de veces la del Sol, los astrónomos han predicho que si este agujero negro se traga otra estrella, producirá un haz de rayos y de repente aclarar. Sin embargo, recientemente, un equipo de investigación conjunto de seis países ha demostrado que esta predicción es cierta. Según la observación actual, cuando una estrella se acerca a un agujero negro, la estrella se hace añicos por la acción de una fuerza gravitatoria muy fuerte. A medida que las piezas rotas de la estrella son absorbidas por el agujero negro, un haz de rayos se dirige en una dirección específica, generando una luz poderosa. Es muy peligroso si este haz de rayos se emite hacia la Tierra. Sin embargo, la probabilidad de que ocurra tal evento es muy baja, de modo que solo ocurre una vez en 100 mil millones de años.

g_text_translate.id:
Dalam banyak kasus, prediksi ilmiah seperti ini tetap menjadi misteri yang belum terpecahkan. Namun, baru-baru ini, prediksi ilmiah 37 tahun yang lalu telah diverifikasi melalui pengamatan yang sebenarnya. Pada jarak 30.000 tahun cahaya dari Bumi, terdapat sebuah lubang hitam besar dengan massa 4,6 juta kali Matahari, para astronom telah meramalkan bahwa jika lubang hitam ini menelan bintang lain, ia akan menghasilkan seikat sinar dan tiba-tiba menerangi. Namun baru-baru ini, prediksi tersebut terbukti benar oleh tim peneliti gabungan dari enam negara. Menurut pengamatan sebenarnya, ketika sebuah bintang mendekati lubang hitam, bintang itu hancur oleh aksi gaya gravitasi yang sangat kuat. Saat potongan-potongan bintang yang hancur tersedot ke dalam lubang hitam, seikat sinar diarahkan ke arah tertentu, menghasilkan cahaya yang kuat. Sangat berbahaya jika berkas sinar ini dipancarkan ke arah Bumi. Namun, kemungkinan terjadinya peristiwa seperti itu sangat rendah, sehingga hanya terjadi sekali dalam 100 miliar tahun.

g_text_translate.ja:
多くの場合、このような科学的予測は未解決の謎のままです。しかし最近、37年前の科学的予測が実際の観測によって検証されました。地球から 30,000 光年の距離に、太陽の 460 万倍の質量を持つ巨大なブラック ホールが存在します。天文学者は、このブラック ホールが別の星を飲み込むと、光線の束が生成され、突然発生すると予測しています。明るくする。しかし最近、この予測は6カ国の共同研究チームによって真実であることが判明しました。実際の観測によると、星がブラックホールに近づくと、星は非常に強い重力の作用によって粉砕されます。粉々になった星の破片がブラック ホールに吸い込まれると、光線の束が特定の方向に向けられ、強力な光が生成されます。この光線の束が地球に向かって放出されると非常に危険です。ただし、そのようなイベントが発生する確率は非常に低く、1000 億年に 1 回しか発生しません。

g_text_translate.de:
In vielen Fällen bleiben wissenschaftliche Vorhersagen wie diese ein ungelöstes Rätsel. Vor kurzem wurden jedoch wissenschaftliche Vorhersagen von vor 37 Jahren durch tatsächliche Beobachtungen bestätigt. In einer Entfernung von 30.000 Lichtjahren von der Erde befindet sich ein massives Schwarzes Loch mit einer Masse von 4,6 Millionen Sonnenmassen. Astronomen haben vorhergesagt, dass dieses Schwarze Loch, wenn es einen anderen Stern verschluckt, plötzlich ein Strahlenbündel erzeugen wird aufhellen. Kürzlich hat sich diese Vorhersage jedoch von einem gemeinsamen Forschungsteam aus sechs Ländern als wahr herausgestellt. Wenn sich ein Stern einem Schwarzen Loch nähert, wird der Stern nach tatsächlicher Beobachtung durch die Wirkung einer sehr starken Gravitationskraft zerschmettert. Wenn die zersplitterten Teile des Sterns in das Schwarze Loch gesaugt werden, wird ein Strahlenbündel in eine bestimmte Richtung gelenkt und erzeugt ein starkes Licht. Es ist sehr gefährlich, wenn dieses Strahlenbündel in Richtung Erde emittiert wird. Die Wahrscheinlichkeit, dass ein solches Ereignis eintritt, ist jedoch sehr gering, so dass es nur einmal in 100 Milliarden Jahren vorkommt.

#######

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
在许多情况下，像这样的科学预测仍然是一个未解之谜。然而，最近，37年前的科学预测已经通过实际观察得到验证。在距离地球3万光年的地方，有一个质量为太阳460万倍的大质量黑洞，天文学家预测，如果这个黑洞吞噬了另一颗恒星，它会产生一束光线，突然间变亮。然而，最近，来自六个国家的联合研究小组证明了这一预测是正确的。根据实际观察，当一颗恒星接近黑洞时，恒星会在非常强的引力作用下破碎。当恒星的碎片被吸入黑洞时，一束光线被引导到特定的方向，产生强大的光芒。如果这束射线射向地球，那是非常危险的。但是，这种事件发生的概率非常低，1000亿年才发生一次。

g_text_translate.it:
In molti casi, previsioni scientifiche come questa rimangono un mistero irrisolto. Tuttavia, recentemente, le previsioni scientifiche di 37 anni fa sono state verificate attraverso osservazioni reali. A una distanza di 30.000 anni luce dalla Terra, c'è un enorme buco nero con una massa di 4,6 milioni di volte quella del Sole, gli astronomi hanno previsto che se questo buco nero inghiottisse un'altra stella, produrrà un fascio di raggi e improvvisamente illuminare. Recentemente, tuttavia, questa previsione si è rivelata vera da un gruppo di ricerca congiunto di sei paesi. Secondo l'osservazione reale, quando una stella si avvicina a un buco nero, la stella viene distrutta dall'azione di una forza gravitazionale molto forte. Mentre i pezzi frantumati della stella vengono risucchiati nel buco nero, un fascio di raggi viene diretto in una direzione specifica, generando una luce potente. È molto pericoloso se questo fascio di raggi viene emesso verso la Terra. Tuttavia, la probabilità che si verifichi un tale evento è molto bassa, tanto che accade solo una volta ogni 100 miliardi di anni.

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
Во многих случаях подобные научные предсказания остаются неразгаданной загадкой. Однако недавно научные предсказания 37-летней давности были подтверждены реальными наблюдениями. На расстоянии 30 000 световых лет от Земли находится массивная черная дыра с массой в 4,6 миллиона раз больше солнечной. Астрономы предсказали, что если эта черная дыра поглотит другую звезду, она произведет пучок лучей и внезапно ярче. Когда осколки звезды засасываются в черную дыру, пучок лучей направляется в определенном направлении, генерируя мощный свет. Очень опасно, если этот пучок лучей будет испущен в сторону Земли. Однако вероятность такого события очень мала: оно происходит только один раз в 100 миллиардов лет.

ในหลายกรณี การพยากรณ์ทางวิทยาศาสตร์เช่นนี้ยังคงเป็นปริศนาที่ยังไม่ได้รับการแก้ไข อย่างไรก็ตาม เมื่อเร็วๆ นี้ การคาดการณ์ทางวิทยาศาสตร์เมื่อ 37 ปีที่แล้วได้รับการยืนยันผ่านการสังเกตจริง ที่ระยะห่างจากโลก 30,000 ปีแสง มีหลุมดำขนาดใหญ่ที่มีมวล 4.6 ล้านเท่าของดวงอาทิตย์ นักดาราศาสตร์คาดการณ์ว่าหากหลุมดำนี้กลืนดาวฤกษ์อีกดวงหนึ่ง มันจะก่อให้เกิดกลุ่มรังสีและทันใดนั้น สดใสขึ้น อย่างไรก็ตาม เมื่อเร็วๆ นี้ คำทำนายนี้กลายเป็นจริงโดยทีมวิจัยร่วมจาก 6 ประเทศ จากการสังเกตจริง เมื่อดาวฤกษ์เข้าใกล้หลุมดำ ดาวจะแตกสลายด้วยการกระทำของแรงโน้มถ่วงที่รุนแรงมาก ขณะที่ชิ้นส่วนที่แตกกระจายของดาวฤกษ์ถูกดูดเข้าไปในหลุมดำ กลุ่มรังสีก็พุ่งไปในทิศทางเฉพาะ ทำให้เกิดแสงอันทรงพลัง เป็นอันตรายมากหากปล่อยรังสีกลุ่มนี้เข้าสู่โลก อย่างไรก็ตาม ความน่าจะเป็นที่เหตุการณ์ดังกล่าวจะเกิดขึ้นนั้นต่ำมาก โดยจะเกิดขึ้นเพียงครั้งเดียวใน 100 พันล้านปี

g_text_translate.pt:
Em muitos casos, previsões científicas como esta permanecem um mistério sem solução. No entanto, recentemente, as previsões científicas de há 37 anos foram verificadas através de observações reais. A uma distância de 30.000 anos-luz da Terra, existe um buraco negro massivo com uma massa de 4,6 milhões de vezes a do Sol. Os astrónomos previram que se este buraco negro engolir outra estrela, produzirá um feixe de raios e de repente iluminar. Recentemente, no entanto, esta previsão revelou-se verdadeira por uma equipa de investigação conjunta de seis países. De acordo com observações reais, quando uma estrela se aproxima de um buraco negro, a estrela é destruída pela ação de uma força gravitacional muito forte. À medida que os pedaços despedaçados da estrela são sugados para dentro do buraco negro, um feixe de raios é direcionado numa direção específica, gerando uma luz poderosa. É muito perigoso se este feixe de raios for emitido em direção à Terra. No entanto, a probabilidade de tal evento ocorrer é muito baixa, de tal forma que só acontece uma vez em 100 mil milhões de anos.

g_text_translate.hi:
कई मामलों में, इस तरह की वैज्ञानिक भविष्यवाणियाँ एक अनसुलझा रहस्य बनी हुई हैं। हालाँकि, हाल ही में, 37 साल पहले की वैज्ञानिक भविष्यवाणियों को वास्तविक अवलोकनों के माध्यम से सत्यापित किया गया है। पृथ्वी से 30,000 प्रकाश वर्ष की दूरी पर, एक विशाल ब्लैक होल है जिसका द्रव्यमान सूर्य से 4.6 मिलियन गुना अधिक है, खगोलविदों ने भविष्यवाणी की है कि यदि यह ब्लैक होल किसी अन्य तारे को निगल जाएगा, तो यह किरणों का एक बंडल उत्पन्न करेगा और अचानक चमकाना. हालाँकि, हाल ही में छह देशों की एक संयुक्त शोध टीम ने इस भविष्यवाणी को सच साबित कर दिया है। वास्तविक अवलोकन के अनुसार, जब कोई तारा किसी ब्लैक होल के पास पहुंचता है, तो बहुत मजबूत गुरुत्वाकर्षण बल की कार्रवाई से तारा टूट जाता है। जैसे ही तारे के टूटे हुए टुकड़े ब्लैक होल में खींचे जाते हैं, किरणों का एक बंडल एक विशिष्ट दिशा में निर्देशित होता है, जिससे एक शक्तिशाली प्रकाश उत्पन्न होता है। यदि किरणों का यह बंडल पृथ्वी की ओर उत्सर्जित होता है तो यह बहुत खतरनाक होता है। हालाँकि, ऐसी घटना घटित होने की संभावना बहुत कम है, जैसे कि यह 100 अरब वर्षों में केवल एक बार होती है।

g_text_translate.tr:
Çoğu durumda bunun gibi bilimsel tahminler çözülmemiş bir gizem olarak kalıyor. Ancak son zamanlarda 37 yıl önceki bilimsel tahminler, gerçek gözlemlerle doğrulandı. Dünya'dan 30.000 ışıkyılı uzaklıkta, Güneş'in 4,6 milyon katı kütleye sahip devasa bir kara delik var. Gökbilimciler, bu kara deliğin başka bir yıldızı yutması durumunda bir ışın demeti üreteceğini ve aniden ortaya çıkacağını tahmin ediyorlardı. aydınlatmak. Ancak son zamanlarda altı ülkeden ortak bir araştırma ekibi tarafından bu öngörünün doğru olduğu ortaya çıktı. Gerçek gözlemlere göre, bir yıldız kara deliğe yaklaştığında, çok güçlü bir çekim kuvvetinin etkisiyle yıldız parçalanır. Yıldızın parçalanan parçaları kara deliğin içine çekilirken, bir ışın demeti belirli bir yöne yönlendirilerek güçlü bir ışık üretir. Bu ışın demetinin Dünya'ya doğru yayılması çok tehlikelidir. Ancak böyle bir olayın gerçekleşme olasılığı çok düşüktür, öyle ki bu olay 100 milyar yılda bir gerçekleşir.

4. ในกรณีที่ทฤษฎีทางวิทยาศาสตร์ได้รับการพิสูจน์แล้วมีผลลัพธ์ที่ขัดแย้งกัน
--------------------
ประโยคแรกบอกว่ายังมีคำทำนายทางวิทยาศาสตร์มากมายที่ยังคงเป็นปริศนาที่ยังไม่ได้รับการแก้ไข คำว่า "แบบนี้" บ่งบอกว่าเนื้อหาก่อนหน้านี้ต้องอ้างอิงถึงการทำนายทางวิทยาศาสตร์ที่ยังไม่มีคำตอบ 3 คือคำตอบที่ถูกต้อง

#######

What is the content before this paragraph?
1. Various scientific predictions of past eras
2. Unbelievable miraculous scientific phenomenon
3. Untested scientific hypotheses
4. Where scientific theories are proven with contradictory results
--------------------
The first sentence says that there are many scientific predictions remain such unsolved mysteries. The word "like this" indicates that the previous content must refer to unanswered scientific predictions. 3 is the correct answer.

4. Onde as teorias científicas são comprovadas com resultados contraditórios

q_answer: 블랙홀의 별 파괴 현상은 아직 사실로 밝혀지지 않고 있다.
블랙홀이 빛나는 현상은 질량이 거대하기 때문에 일어난다.
블랙홀로 빨려 들어간 별은 결국 여러 조각으로 부서지게 된다.
블랙홀은 별의 잔해들을 빨아들일 때 광선 다발을 분출하게 된다.

2. Hiện tượng lỗ đen phát ra ánh sáng xảy ra do khối lượng lớn. 
3. Ngôi sao bị hút vào lỗ đen cuối cùng bị phá vỡ thành nhiều mảnh. 
4. Lỗ đen phun ra bó ánh sáng khi chúng hút các tàn dư của ngôi sao.
--------------------
Trọng lực mạnh của lỗ đen khiến ngôi sao đến gần bị vỡ vụn. Do đó 3 là đáp án đúng.

2. The phenomenon of black holes emitting light occurs due to high mass.
3. The star that is sucked into the black hole is eventually broken into pieces.
4. Black holes spit out bundles of light as they suck up the remnants of stars.
--------------------
The strong gravity of the black hole causes the nearby star to crumble. So 3 is the correct answer.

general.g_text:
전 세계적으로 고래는 약 80여 종이 서식하는데 일부 지역에서는 자연 상태의 고래를 관찰할 수 있는 생태 고래 관광이 행해지고 있다. 고래 관광사업자들은 고래를 생존 상태로 활용하는 것이 (        ) 해양 생물 보호라는 측면에서도 의미가 있다고 주장한다. 환경 운동가들도 고래개체 수가 감소하는 상황에서 고래 관광이 포경 사업보다 경제적 측면에서나 환경 보호 측면에서 모두 유익하다며 이에 동조하고 있다. 
그러나 각종 시설과 유조선, 기후 변화 등으로 해양 생태계가 악화되는 현 시점에서, 고래를 관광 상품으로 활용하는 것이 생태 자원을 과연 올바르게 보전하는 것인가에 대해서는 아직 논란의 여지가 있다. 실제로 고래 관광으로 인해 고래의 행동 양식이나 이동 방향, 번식 주기 등의 변화가 생겼다는 보고가 있기 때문이다.

g_text_translate.vi:
Có khoảng 80 loài cá voi sinh sống trên toàn thế giới, một số khu vực đang tiến hành du lịch cá voi sinh thái để có thể quan sát cá voi ở trạng thái tự nhiên. Các nhà khai thác du lịch cá voi cho rằng việc sử dụng cá voi ở trạng thái sinh tồn (        ) mà còn về mặt bảo vệ sinh vật biển. Các nhà hoạt động môi trường cũng đồng tình với việc du lịch cá voi có lợi về mặt kinh tế và bảo vệ môi trường hơn là kinh doanh săn bắt cá voi trong bối cảnh số lượng cá voi giảm. 
Tuy nhiên, vẫn còn tranh cãi về việc liệu việc sử dụng cá voi làm sản phẩm du lịch có bảo tồn tài nguyên sinh thái một cách đúng đắn hay không trong thời điểm hệ sinh thái biển xấu đi do các cơ sở vật chất, tàu chở dầu và biến đổi khí hậu như hiện nay. Bởi vì trên thực tế, du lịch cá voi đã tạo ra những thay đổi trong hành vi, phương hướng di chuyển và chu kỳ sinh sản của cá voi.
g_text_translate.en:
There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.
However, at this point in time when the marine ecosystem is deteriorating due to various facilities, oil tankers, and climate change, there is still controversy over whether using whales as a tourism product preserves ecological resources properly. In fact, there are reports that whale watching has changed the behavior, migration direction, and breeding cycle of whales.
g_text_translate.fr:
Il existe environ 80 espèces de baleines dans le monde et, dans certaines régions, un tourisme éco-baleinier est organisé pour observer les baleines dans leur état naturel. Les opérateurs de tourisme baleinier affirment que l'utilisation de baleines vivantes est non seulement plus rentable que la chasse conventionnelle, mais également significative en termes de protection de la vie marine. Les militants écologistes sont également d'accord avec cela, affirmant que le tourisme baleinier est plus bénéfique que la chasse à la baleine en termes de protection économique et environnementale face au déclin des populations de baleines.
Cependant, à l'heure actuelle où l'écosystème marin se détériore en raison de diverses installations, des pétroliers et du changement climatique, il existe toujours une controverse quant à savoir si l'utilisation des baleines comme produit touristique préserve correctement les ressources écologiques. En fait, il existe des rapports selon lesquels l'observation des baleines a changé le comportement, la direction de la migration et le cycle de reproduction des baleines.

#######

There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.
However, at this point in time when the marine ecosystem is deteriorating due to various facilities, oil tankers, and climate change, there is still controversy over whether using whales as a tourism product preserves ecological resources properly. In fact, there are reports that whale watching has changed the behavior, migration direction, and breeding cycle of whales.
g_text_translate.es:
Hay alrededor de 80 especies de ballenas en todo el mundo, y en algunas áreas se está realizando turismo ecológico de ballenas para observar ballenas en su estado natural. Los operadores de turismo de ballenas afirman que el uso de ballenas en estado vivo no solo es más rentable que la caza convencional de ballenas, sino que también es significativo en términos de protección de la vida marina. Los activistas ambientales también están de acuerdo con esto y dicen que el turismo de ballenas es más beneficioso que la caza de ballenas en términos de protección económica y ambiental frente a la disminución de las poblaciones de ballenas.
Sin embargo, en este momento en que el ecosistema marino se está deteriorando debido a diversas instalaciones, petroleros y el cambio climático, todavía existe controversia sobre si el uso de ballenas como producto turístico preserva adecuadamente los recursos ecológicos. De hecho, hay informes de que la observación de ballenas ha cambiado el comportamiento, la dirección de la migración y el ciclo de reproducción de las ballenas.

#######

g_text_translate.id:
Ada sekitar 80 spesies paus di seluruh dunia, dan di beberapa daerah, pariwisata paus ramah lingkungan sedang dilakukan untuk mengamati paus dalam keadaan alaminya. Operator wisata paus mengklaim bahwa penggunaan paus dalam keadaan hidup tidak hanya lebih menguntungkan daripada penangkapan ikan paus konvensional, tetapi juga bermakna dalam hal melindungi kehidupan laut. Aktivis lingkungan juga setuju dengan hal ini, mengatakan bahwa wisata paus lebih bermanfaat daripada perburuan paus baik dari segi ekonomi dan perlindungan lingkungan dalam menghadapi populasi paus yang menurun.
Namun, pada saat ini ketika ekosistem laut memburuk karena berbagai fasilitas, kapal tanker minyak, dan perubahan iklim, masih ada kontroversi apakah menggunakan paus sebagai produk wisata melestarikan sumber daya ekologis dengan baik. Bahkan, ada laporan bahwa wisata mengamati paus telah mengubah perilaku, arah migrasi, dan siklus perkembangbiakan paus.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
世界には約80種のクジラが生息しており、一部の地域ではクジラを自然の姿で観察するエコクジラツーリズムが行われています。クジラ観光事業者は、クジラを生きた状態で利用することは、従来の捕鯨よりも収益性が高いだけでなく、海洋生物を保護するという意味でも意味があると主張しています。環境活動家もこれに同意し、クジラの個体数の減少に直面している経済と環境保護の両方の観点から、クジラの観光は捕鯨よりも有益であると述べています.
しかし、さまざまな施設や石油タンカー、気候変動などにより、海洋生態系が悪化している現在、クジラを観光商品として利用することが生態系資源を適切に保護するかどうかについては、依然として議論の余地があります。実際、ホエール ウォッチングによってクジラの行動、回遊方向、繁殖サイクルが変化したという報告もあります。

g_text_translate.de:
Es gibt etwa 80 Walarten auf der ganzen Welt, und in einigen Gebieten wird Öko-Waltourismus betrieben, um Wale in ihrem natürlichen Zustand zu beobachten. Waltourismusbetreiber behaupten, dass der Einsatz von Walen in lebendem Zustand nicht nur rentabler als der konventionelle Walfang, sondern auch sinnvoll für den Schutz der Meereslebewesen ist. Dem stimmen auch Umweltaktivisten zu, die sagen, dass der Waltourismus angesichts schrumpfender Walpopulationen sowohl wirtschaftlich als auch umweltschonend vorteilhafter ist als der Walfang.
Zu diesem Zeitpunkt, an dem sich das Meeresökosystem aufgrund verschiedener Anlagen, Öltanker und des Klimawandels verschlechtert, wird jedoch immer noch kontrovers darüber diskutiert, ob die Nutzung von Walen als Tourismusprodukt die ökologischen Ressourcen angemessen schützt. Tatsächlich gibt es Berichte, dass das Beobachten von Walen das Verhalten, die Zugrichtung und den Brutzyklus von Walen verändert hat.

#######

There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
全世界大约有 80 种鲸鱼，在一些地区，正在开展生态鲸鱼旅游，以观察鲸鱼的自然状态。鲸鱼旅游经营者声称，在活体状态下使用鲸鱼不仅比传统捕鲸更有利可图，而且在保护海洋生物方面也很有意义。环保人士也同意这一点，称面对鲸鱼数量的减少，从经济和环境保护两方面来说，鲸鱼旅游比捕鲸更有益。
然而，在海洋生态系统因各种设施、油轮和气候变化而恶化的当下，将鲸鱼作为旅游产品是否能妥善保护生态资源仍存在争议。事实上，有报道称观鲸改变了鲸鱼的行为、迁徙方向和繁殖周期。

In fact, there are reports that whale watching has changed the behavior, migration direction, and breeding cycle of whales.
g_text_translate.ru:
В мире насчитывается около 80 видов китов, а в некоторых районах проводится экокитовый туризм с целью наблюдения за китами в их естественном состоянии. Операторы китового туризма утверждают, что использование китов в живом состоянии не только более выгодно, чем обычный китобойный промысел, но и значимо с точки зрения защиты морской жизни. Активисты-экологи также согласны с этим, заявляя, что китовый туризм более выгоден, чем китобойный промысел, с точки зрения как экономической, так и экологической защиты в условиях сокращения популяции китов.
Однако на данный момент, когда морская экосистема ухудшается из-за различных объектов, нефтяных танкеров и изменения климата, все еще существуют споры о том, сохраняет ли использование китов в качестве туристического продукта должным образом экологические ресурсы. Фактически, есть сообщения о том, что наблюдение за китами изменило поведение, направление миграции и цикл размножения китов.

#######

There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.
However, at this point in time when the marine ecosystem is deteriorating due to various facilities, oil tankers, and climate change, there is still controversy over whether using whales as a tourism product preserves ecological resources properly. In fact, there are reports that whale watching has changed the behavior, migration direction, and breeding cycle of whales.
g_text_translate.th:
มีวาฬประมาณ 80 สายพันธุ์ทั่วโลก และในบางพื้นที่ก็มีการท่องเที่ยววาฬเชิงนิเวศเพื่อชมวาฬในสภาพธรรมชาติ ผู้ประกอบการท่องเที่ยววาฬอ้างว่าการใช้วาฬในสภาพมีชีวิตไม่เพียงแต่ให้ผลกำไรมากกว่าการล่าวาฬทั่วไปเท่านั้น แต่ยังมีความหมายในแง่ของการปกป้องสิ่งมีชีวิตใต้ท้องทะเลด้วย นักเคลื่อนไหวด้านสิ่งแวดล้อมยังเห็นด้วยกับสิ่งนี้ โดยกล่าวว่าการท่องเที่ยววาฬมีประโยชน์มากกว่าการล่าวาฬทั้งในแง่ของการปกป้องทางเศรษฐกิจและสิ่งแวดล้อม เมื่อเผชิญกับจำนวนวาฬที่ลดลง
อย่างไรก็ตาม ณ จุดนี้ที่ระบบนิเวศทางทะเลเสื่อมโทรมลงเนื่องจากสิ่งอำนวยความสะดวกต่างๆ เรือบรรทุกน้ำมัน และการเปลี่ยนแปลงสภาพภูมิอากาศ ยังคงมีข้อถกเถียงกันว่าการใช้วาฬเป็นผลิตภัณฑ์การท่องเที่ยวจะช่วยรักษาทรัพยากรทางนิเวศได้อย่างเหมาะสมหรือไม่ ที่จริงแล้วมีรายงานว่าการดูวาฬได้เปลี่ยนแปลงพฤติกรรม ทิศทางการอพยพ และวงจรการผสมพันธุ์ของวาฬ

#######

There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.
However, at this point in time when the marine ecosystem is deteriorating due to various facilities, oil tankers, and climate change, there is still controversy over whether using whales as a tourism product preserves ecological resources properly. g_text_translate.pt:
Existem cerca de 80 espécies de baleias em todo o mundo e, em algumas áreas, o turismo ecológico de baleias está sendo realizado para observar as baleias em seu estado natural. Os operadores de turismo baleeiro afirmam que a utilização de baleias vivas não é apenas mais lucrativa do que a caça convencional, mas também significativa em termos de protecção da vida marinha. Os activistas ambientais também concordam com isto, dizendo que o turismo baleeiro é mais benéfico do que a caça às baleias em termos de protecção económica e ambiental face ao declínio das populações de baleias.
No entanto, neste momento em que o ecossistema marinho está a deteriorar-se devido a várias instalações, petroleiros e alterações climáticas, ainda há controvérsia sobre se a utilização das baleias como produto turístico preserva adequadamente os recursos ecológicos. Na verdade, há relatos de que a observação de baleias mudou o comportamento, a direção da migração e o ciclo reprodutivo das baleias.

#######

There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.
However, at this point in time when the marine ecosystem is deteriorating due to various facilities, oil tankers, and climate change, there is still controversy over whether using whales as a tourism product preserves ecological resources properly. In fact, there are reports that whale watching has changed the behavior, migration direction, and breeding cycle of whales.
g_text_translate.hi:
दुनिया भर में व्हेल की लगभग 80 प्रजातियाँ हैं, और कुछ क्षेत्रों में, व्हेल को उनकी प्राकृतिक अवस्था में देखने के लिए इको-व्हेल पर्यटन आयोजित किया जा रहा है। व्हेल पर्यटन संचालकों का दावा है कि जीवित अवस्था में व्हेल का उपयोग न केवल पारंपरिक व्हेलिंग की तुलना में अधिक लाभदायक है, बल्कि समुद्री जीवन की रक्षा के लिहाज से भी सार्थक है। पर्यावरण कार्यकर्ता भी इससे सहमत हैं, उनका कहना है कि व्हेल की घटती आबादी के मद्देनजर आर्थिक और पर्यावरण संरक्षण दोनों की दृष्टि से व्हेल पर्यटन व्हेल शिकार से अधिक फायदेमंद है।
हालाँकि, इस समय जब विभिन्न सुविधाओं, तेल टैंकरों और जलवायु परिवर्तन के कारण समुद्री पारिस्थितिकी तंत्र बिगड़ रहा है, तब भी इस बात पर विवाद है कि व्हेल को पर्यटन उत्पाद के रूप में उपयोग करने से पारिस्थितिक संसाधनों का उचित संरक्षण होता है या नहीं। दरअसल, ऐसी रिपोर्टें हैं कि व्हेल देखने से व्हेल के व्यवहार, प्रवास की दिशा और प्रजनन चक्र में बदलाव आया है।

#######

There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.
However, at this point in time when the marine ecosystem is deteriorating due to various facilities, oil tankers, and climate change, there is still controversy over whether using whales as a tourism product preserves ecological resources properly. In fact, there are reports that whale watching has changed the behavior, migration direction, and breeding cycle of whales.
g_text_translate.tr:
Dünya çapında 80'e yakın balina türü bulunuyor ve bazı bölgelerde balinaları doğal halleriyle gözlemlemek amacıyla eko-balina turizmi yapılıyor. Balina turizmi işletmecileri, balinaların canlı halde kullanılmasının, geleneksel balina avcılığına göre daha karlı olmasının yanı sıra deniz yaşamının korunması açısından da anlamlı olduğunu iddia ediyor. Çevre aktivistleri de balina popülasyonlarının azalması karşısında hem ekonomik hem de çevresel koruma açısından balina turizminin balina avcılığından daha faydalı olduğunu söyleyerek bu görüşe katılıyor.
Ancak çeşitli tesisler, petrol tankerleri ve iklim değişikliği nedeniyle deniz ekosisteminin bozulduğu bu dönemde, balinaların turizm ürünü olarak kullanılmasının ekolojik kaynakları yeterince koruyup korumadığı konusunda tartışmalar sürüyor. Hatta balina izlemenin balinaların davranışını, göç yönünü ve üreme döngüsünü değiştirdiğine dair raporlar var.

#######

There are about 80 species of whales around the world, and in some areas, eco-whale tourism is being conducted to observe whales in their natural state. Whale tourism operators claim that the use of whales in a live state is not only more profitable than conventional whaling, but also meaningful in terms of protecting marine life. Environmental activists also agree with this, saying that whale tourism is more beneficial than whaling in terms of both economic and environmental protection in the face of declining whale populations.
However, at this point in time when the marine ecosystem is deteriorating due to various facilities, oil tankers, and climate change, there is still controversy over whether using whales as a tourism product preserves ecological resources properly. In fact, there are reports that whale watching has changed the behavior, migration direction, and breeding cycle of whales.

general.g_text:
이와 같은 발견으로 2009년에 미국과 유럽 연합은 토성의 두 위성에 새로운 우주선을 보내는 계획을 발표했다. 이 계획의 1차 목적은 토성의 위성인 타이탄의 대기와 또 다른 위성 엥켈라두스의 땅속을 조사함으로써 이 위성들이 어떻게 만들어졌으며 어떤 변화가 있었는지를 밝히는 것이다. 타이탄은 대기를 가진 것으로 예상되고 엥켈라두스는 땅속에 바다가 있는 것으로 추측된다. 이 위성들은 생물이 존재할 가능성이 있는 위성들로서 결국 이 계획의 최종 목적은 (        ) 데 있는 셈이다. 
이 계획은 2020년 우주선 발사를 목표로 하고 있으며 발사 9년 후 토성 근처에 도착할 예정이다. 그 후 4년에 걸친 기본 조사가 끝나고 기계나 전송 시스템 등에 문제가 없다면 조사를 더 연장할 것으로 보인다.

g_text_translate.vi:
Với những khám phá này, vào năm 2009, Hoa Kỳ và Liên minh Châu Âu đã công bố kế hoạch gửi tàu vũ trụ mới đến hai vệ tinh của sao Thổ. Mục đích 1 của kế hoạch là điều tra bầu khí quyển của vệ tinh Titan của sao Thổ và trong lòng đất của một vệ tinh khác là Enceladus để xác định cách những vệ tinh này được hình thành và những thay đổi nào đã xảy ra. Titan được cho là có bầu khí quyển, và Enceladus được cho là có đại dương dưới lòng đất. Những vệ tinh này là những vệ tinh có khả năng có sự sống tồn tại, nên mục tiêu cuối cùng của kế hoạch này là (        ).
Kế hoạch này đặt mục tiêu phóng tàu vũ trụ vào năm 2020 và dự kiến ​​sẽ đến gần sao Thổ 9 năm sau khi phóng. Sau đó, nếu cuộc khảo sát cơ bản trong 4 năm kết thúc và không có vấn đề gì về máy móc hoặc hệ thống truyền dẫn, cuộc khảo sát dự kiến ​​sẽ được kéo dài thêm.
g_text_translate.en:
With these discoveries, in 2009 the United States and the European Union announced plans to send new spacecraft to two moons of Saturn. The primary purpose of the plan is to investigate the atmosphere of Saturn's moon Titan and the subsurface of another moon, Enceladus, to determine how these moons were formed and what changes occurred. Titan is expected to have an atmosphere, and Enceladus is believed to have an underground ocean. These moons are potentially life-like satellites, and the final purpose of the plan is to search for life outside the Earth.
The plan aims to launch the spacecraft in 2020 and is expected to arrive near Saturn nine years after launch. After that, if the basic investigation over 4 years is over and there are no problems with the machine or transmission system, the investigation is expected to be extended further.
g_text_translate.fr:
Avec ces découvertes, en 2009, les États-Unis et l'Union européenne ont annoncé leur intention d'envoyer de nouveaux engins spatiaux sur deux lunes de Saturne. L'objectif principal du plan est d'étudier l'atmosphère de Titan, la lune de Saturne, et le sous-sol d'une autre lune, Encelade, afin de déterminer comment ces lunes se sont formées et quels changements se sont produits. On s'attend à ce que Titan ait une atmosphère et qu'Encelade ait un océan souterrain. Ces lunes sont des satellites potentiellement vivants, et le but final du plan est de rechercher la vie en dehors de la Terre.
Le plan vise à lancer le vaisseau spatial en 2020 et devrait arriver près de Saturne neuf ans après son lancement. Après cela, si l'enquête de base sur 4 ans est terminée et qu'il n'y a aucun problème avec la machine ou le système de transmission, l'enquête devrait être prolongée.

#######

These moons are potentially life-like satellites, and the final purpose of the plan is to search for life outside the Earth.

g_text_translate.es:
Con estos descubrimientos, en 2009 Estados Unidos y la Unión Europea anunciaron planes para enviar nuevas naves espaciales a dos lunas de Saturno. El propósito principal del plan es investigar la atmósfera de Titán, la luna de Saturno, y el subsuelo de otra luna, Encelado, para determinar cómo se formaron estas lunas y qué cambios ocurrieron. Se espera que Titán tenga una atmósfera y se cree que Encelado tiene un océano subterráneo. Estas lunas son satélites potencialmente similares a la vida, y el propósito final del plan es buscar vida fuera de la Tierra.
El plan apunta a lanzar la nave espacial en 2020 y se espera que llegue cerca de Saturno nueve años después del lanzamiento. Después de eso, si la investigación básica de 4 años finaliza y no hay problemas con la máquina o el sistema de transmisión, se espera que la investigación se extienda más.

g_text_translate.id:
Dengan penemuan ini, pada tahun 2009 Amerika Serikat dan Uni Eropa mengumumkan rencana untuk mengirim pesawat ruang angkasa baru ke dua bulan Saturnus. Tujuan utama dari rencana ini adalah untuk menyelidiki atmosfer bulan Saturnus, Titan dan bawah permukaan bulan lain, Enceladus, untuk menentukan bagaimana bulan-bulan ini terbentuk dan perubahan apa yang terjadi. Titan diperkirakan memiliki atmosfer, dan Enceladus diyakini memiliki lautan bawah tanah. Bulan-bulan ini berpotensi menjadi satelit yang mirip kehidupan, dan tujuan akhir dari rencana tersebut adalah untuk mencari kehidupan di luar Bumi.
Rencana tersebut bertujuan untuk meluncurkan pesawat ruang angkasa pada tahun 2020 dan diharapkan tiba di dekat Saturnus sembilan tahun setelah peluncuran. Setelah itu, jika pemeriksaan dasar selama 4 tahun sudah selesai dan tidak ada masalah dengan mesin atau sistem transmisi, maka pemeriksaan tersebut diharapkan dapat diperpanjang lebih lanjut.

g_text_translate.ja:
これらの発見により、2009 年に米国と欧州連合は、土星の 2 つの衛星に新しい宇宙船を送る計画を発表しました。この計画の主な目的は、土星の衛星タイタンの大気と別の衛星エンケラドゥスの地下を調査して、これらの衛星がどのように形成され、どのような変化が起こったかを明らかにすることです。タイタンには大気があり、エンケラドゥスには地下海があると考えられています。これらの月は生命に似た衛星である可能性があり、計画の最終的な目的は地球外の生命を探すことです。
計画は2020年に宇宙船を打ち上げることを目指しており、打ち上げから9年後に土星の近くに到着する予定です。その後、4年にわたる基礎調査が終了し、機械や伝達系に問題がなければ、さらに調査が延長される見込みです。

g_text_translate.de:
Aufgrund dieser Entdeckungen gaben die Vereinigten Staaten und die Europäische Union 2009 Pläne bekannt, neue Raumfahrzeuge zu zwei Saturnmonden zu schicken. Es wird erwartet, dass Titan eine Atmosphäre hat, und Enceladus soll einen unterirdischen Ozean haben. g_text_translate.zh-CN:
有了这些发现，美国和欧盟在 2009 年宣布了向土星的两个卫星发射新航天器的计划。该计划的主要目的是调查土星卫星泰坦的大气和另一个卫星土卫二的地下，以确定这些卫星是如何形成的以及发生了什么变化。土卫六预计有大气层，而土卫二被认为有地下海洋。这些卫星是潜在的类生命卫星，该计划的最终目的是寻找地球以外的生命。

g_text_translate.it:

Lo scopo principale del piano è indagare l'atmosfera della luna di Saturno, Titano, e il sottosuolo di un'altra luna, Encelado, per determinare come si sono formate queste lune e quali cambiamenti si sono verificati. Si prevede che Titano abbia un'atmosfera e che Encelado abbia un oceano sotterraneo. Queste lune sono potenzialmente satelliti simili alla vita e lo scopo finale del piano è cercare la vita al di fuori della Terra.

#######

With these discoveries, in 2009 the United States and the European Union announced plans to send new spacecraft to two moons of Saturn. The primary purpose of the plan is to investigate the atmosphere of Saturn's moon Titan and the subsurface of another moon, Enceladus, to determine how these moons were formed and what changes occurred. Titan is expected to have an atmosphere, and Enceladus is believed to have an underground ocean. These moons are potentially life-like satellites, and the final purpose of the plan is to search for life outside the Earth.
The plan aims to launch the spacecraft in 2020 and is expected to arrive near Saturn nine years after launch. After that, if the basic investigation over 4 years is over and there are no problems with the machine or transmission system, the investigation is expected to be extended further.
g_text_translate.ru:
Благодаря этим открытиям в 2009 году Соединенные Штаты и Европейский Союз объявили о планах отправить новые космические корабли к двум спутникам Сатурна. Основная цель плана — исследовать атмосферу спутника Сатурна Титана и недр другого спутника, Энцелада, чтобы определить, как образовались эти спутники и какие изменения произошли. Ожидается, что у Титана будет атмосфера, а у Энцелада — подземный океан. Эти спутники потенциально являются спутниками, похожими на жизнь, и конечной целью плана является поиск жизни за пределами Земли.
План направлен на запуск космического корабля в 2020 году, и ожидается, что он прибудет к Сатурну через девять лет после запуска. После этого, если базовое расследование, длившееся более 4 лет, завершено и проблем с машиной или системой трансмиссии нет, расследование, как ожидается, будет продлено дальше.

จากการค้นพบเหล่านี้ ในปี พ.ศ. 2552 สหรัฐอเมริกาและสหภาพยุโรปได้ประกาศแผนการส่งยานอวกาศใหม่ไปยังดวงจันทร์สองดวงของดาวเสาร์ วัตถุประสงค์หลักของแผนคือเพื่อตรวจสอบบรรยากาศของดวงจันทร์ไททันของดาวเสาร์และใต้พื้นผิวของดวงจันทร์อีกดวงหนึ่งชื่อเอนเซลาดัส เพื่อพิจารณาว่าดวงจันทร์เหล่านี้ก่อตัวอย่างไรและมีการเปลี่ยนแปลงอะไรบ้าง ไททันคาดว่าจะมีชั้นบรรยากาศ และเอนเซลาดัสเชื่อว่ามีมหาสมุทรใต้ดิน ดวงจันทร์เหล่านี้อาจเป็นดาวเทียมที่มีลักษณะคล้ายมีชีวิต และจุดประสงค์สุดท้ายของแผนนี้คือการค้นหาสิ่งมีชีวิตนอกโลก
แผนดังกล่าวมีเป้าหมายที่จะเปิดตัวยานอวกาศในปี 2563 และคาดว่าจะมาถึงใกล้ดาวเสาร์หลังจากปล่อยยานอวกาศได้เก้าปี หลังจากนั้นหากสิ้นสุดการสอบสวนเบื้องต้นเกิน 4 ปี และไม่มีปัญหากับเครื่องจักรหรือระบบส่งกำลังคาดว่าจะขยายเวลาการสอบสวนต่อไป

g_text_translate.pt:
Com estas descobertas, em 2009 os Estados Unidos e a União Europeia anunciaram planos para enviar novas naves espaciais a duas luas de Saturno. O objetivo principal do plano é investigar a atmosfera da lua de Saturno, Titã, e a subsuperfície de outra lua, Encélado, para determinar como essas luas foram formadas e quais mudanças ocorreram. Espera-se que Titã tenha uma atmosfera e acredita-se que Encélado tenha um oceano subterrâneo. Estas luas são satélites potencialmente semelhantes à vida, e o objetivo final do plano é procurar vida fora da Terra.
O plano visa lançar a espaçonave em 2020 e deverá chegar perto de Saturno nove anos após o lançamento. Depois disso, se a investigação básica de 4 anos terminar e não houver problemas com a máquina ou sistema de transmissão, espera-se que a investigação seja estendida ainda mais.

g_text_translate.hi:
इन खोजों के साथ, 2009 में संयुक्त राज्य अमेरिका और यूरोपीय संघ ने शनि के दो चंद्रमाओं पर नए अंतरिक्ष यान भेजने की योजना की घोषणा की। योजना का प्राथमिक उद्देश्य शनि के चंद्रमा टाइटन के वातावरण और दूसरे चंद्रमा एन्सेलाडस की उपसतह की जांच करना है, ताकि यह निर्धारित किया जा सके कि ये चंद्रमा कैसे बने और क्या परिवर्तन हुए। टाइटन में एक वायुमंडल होने की उम्मीद है, और एन्सेलाडस में एक भूमिगत महासागर होने की उम्मीद है। ये चंद्रमा संभावित रूप से जीवन जैसे उपग्रह हैं, और योजना का अंतिम उद्देश्य पृथ्वी के बाहर जीवन की खोज करना है।
योजना का लक्ष्य 2020 में अंतरिक्ष यान लॉन्च करना है और लॉन्च के नौ साल बाद शनि के पास पहुंचने की उम्मीद है। उसके बाद, यदि 4 वर्षों से अधिक की बुनियादी जांच पूरी हो जाती है और मशीन या ट्रांसमिशन सिस्टम में कोई समस्या नहीं होती है, तो जांच को आगे बढ़ाए जाने की उम्मीद है।

g_text_translate.tr:
Bu keşiflerle birlikte 2009 yılında Amerika Birleşik Devletleri ve Avrupa Birliği, Satürn'ün iki ayına yeni uzay aracı gönderme planlarını duyurdu. Planın temel amacı, Satürn'ün uydusu Titan'ın atmosferini ve diğer uydu Enceladus'un yüzeyini araştırarak bu uyduların nasıl oluştuğunu ve hangi değişikliklerin meydana geldiğini belirlemektir. Titan'ın bir atmosfere sahip olması bekleniyor ve Enceladus'un bir yer altı okyanusuna sahip olduğuna inanılıyor. Bu aylar potansiyel olarak hayata benzeyen uydulardır ve planın nihai amacı Dünya dışında yaşam aramaktır.
Plan, uzay aracının 2020 yılında fırlatılmasını hedefliyor ve fırlatıldıktan dokuz yıl sonra Satürn'ün yakınlarına ulaşması bekleniyor. Bundan sonra 4 yılı aşkın süredir devam eden temel soruşturmanın bitmesi ve makine veya aktarma sisteminde herhangi bir sorun olmaması halinde soruşturmanın daha da uzatılması bekleniyor.

explain.vi:
Nội dung phía trước của đoạn văn này là gì?
1. Kết quả phân tích cho thấy bề mặt của các vệ tinh sao Thổ giống nhau.
2. Kết quả phân tích cho thấy nhiệt độ của các vệ tinh của sao Thổ đang tăng lên
3. Kết quả quan sát cho thấy các vệ tinh của sao Thổ đang đến gần trái đất
4. Kết quả quan sát thu được dấu vết của không khí và nước trên các vệ tinh của sao Thổ
--------------------
Titan được cho là có bầu khí quyển, và Enceladus được cho là có đại dương dưới lòng đất. Như vậy nội dung phía trước cho biết đã quan sát được dấu vết của không khí và nước trên các vệ tinh của sao Thổ. 4 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
What is the content before this paragraph?
1. Analysis results show that the surface of Saturn's moons is similar.
2. Analysis results show that the temperature of Saturn's moons is increasing
3. Observation results show that Saturn's moons are approaching the earth
4. Observation results show traces of air and water on Saturn's moons
--------------------
Titan is thought to have an atmosphere, and Enceladus is thought to have an underground ocean. Thus, the preceding content indicates that traces of air and water have been observed on Saturn's moons. 4 is the correct answer.

3. Les résultats d'observation montrent que les lunes de Saturne se rapprochent de la Terre
4. Les résultats d'observation montrent des traces d'air et d'eau sur les lunes de Saturne
--------------------
On pense que Titan a une atmosphère et qu'Encelade a un océan souterrain. Ainsi, le contenu précédent indique que des traces d'air et d'eau ont été observées sur les lunes de Saturne. 4 est la bonne réponse.

4. ผลการสังเกตพบร่องรอยของอากาศและน้ำบนดวงจันทร์ของดาวเสาร์
--------------------
คิดว่าไททันมีบรรยากาศ และเอนเซลาดัสคิดว่ามีมหาสมุทรใต้ดิน ดังนั้นเนื้อหาข้างต้นจึงบ่งชี้ว่ามีการสังเกตร่องรอยของอากาศและน้ำบนดวงจันทร์ของดาวเสาร์ 4 คือคำตอบที่ถูกต้อง

#######

What is the content before this paragraph?
1. Analysis results show that the surface of Saturn's moons is similar.
2. Analysis results show that the temperature of Saturn's moons is increasing
3. Observation results show that Saturn's moons are approaching the earth
4. Observation results show traces of air and water on Saturn's moons
--------------------
Titan is thought to have an atmosphere, and Enceladus is thought to have an underground ocean. Thus, the preceding content indicates that traces of air and water have been observed on Saturn's moons. 4 is the correct answer.
explain.pt:

1. Os resultados da análise mostram que a superfície das luas de Saturno é semelhante.

3. Os resultados das observações mostram que as luas de Saturno estão se aproximando da Terra
4. Os resultados das observações mostram vestígios de ar e água nas luas de Saturno
--------------------
Acredita-se que Titã tenha uma atmosfera e Encélado tenha um oceano subterrâneo. Assim, o conteúdo anterior indica que foram observados vestígios de ar e água nas luas de Saturno. 4 é a resposta correta.

explain.hi:

1. विश्लेषण के नतीजे बताते हैं कि शनि के चंद्रमाओं की सतह समान है।
2. विश्लेषण के नतीजे बताते हैं कि शनि के चंद्रमाओं का तापमान बढ़ रहा है

content.q_text: 이 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 이 계획은 위성들이 생긴 과정부터 밝힐 것이다.
땅속 조사는 지구에 부족한 자원을 찾기 위한 조사이다.
우주선은 위성의 표면 물질을 수집해 지구로 보낼 것이다.
우주선을 보내는 시기와 연구 기간은 아직 결정되지 않았다.

explain.vi:
Chọn câu giống với nội dung của đoạn văn.
1. Kế hoạch này sẽ làm sáng tỏ bắt đầu từ quá trình hình thành các vệ tinh. 
2. Khảo sát dưới lòng đất là khảo sát để tìm kiếm nguồn tài nguyên thiếu hụt trên trái đất. 
3. Tàu vũ trụ sẽ thu thập vật chất bề mặt của vệ tinh và gửi về trái đất. 
4. Thời gian phóng tàu vũ trụ và thời hạn nghiên cứu vẫn chưa được quyết định.
--------------------
Mục đích 1 của kế hoạch là để xác định cách những vệ tinh này hình thành và những thay đổi đã xảy ra. Do đó 1 là đáp án đúng.

general.g_text:
인류 문명의 발달 과정에서 집단 가무의 발명은 획기적인 사건이었다. 다 함께 소리를 지르고 율동적으로 몸을 움직임으로써 박자를 맞추는 행위는 집단 내에 강력한 협동과 조화를 낳는 놀라운 효과를 발휘했다. 즉 축제 때에 함께 노래하고 춤을 춤으로써 참가자들은 (        ). 또 전쟁 중에 전사들은 집단 가무를 통해 일시적으로 죽음의 공포를 넘고 전체 집단을 지키기 위해 기꺼이 온몸을 던지는 용기를 얻었다. 이처럼 집단 가무는 부족을 하나로 묶어 주는 마법과 같은 힘을 발휘하였다. 
인류 문명이 발전하면서 집단 가무는 종교 의식에 쓰이거나 인간 내면의 섬세한 감성을 표현하는 식으로 훨씬 더 세련되고 고상한 기능을 맡았다. 그럼에도 집단 가무는 여전히 군악대의 행진곡이나 단체 응원 등에 남아 인류 역사 초기에 담당했던 기본적인 역할을 보여 주고 있다.

g_text_translate.vi:
Trong quá trình phát triển của nền văn minh nhân loại, việc phát minh ra ca múa tập thể là một sự kiện mang tính bước ngoặt. Hành động hòa nhịp bằng cách hét lên cùng nhau và di chuyển cơ thể nhịp nhàng phát huy tác dụng đáng ngạc nhiên, tạo ra sự hiệp đồng và hòa hợp mạnh mẽ trong tập thể. Nói cách khác, bằng cách hát và nhảy cùng nhau trong lễ hội, những người tham gia (        ). Ngoài ra, trong chiến tranh, các chiến binh nhất thời vượt qua nỗi sợ hãi về cái chết thông qua ca múa tập thể và có được dũng khí để sẵn sàng gieo mình để bảo vệ tập thể. Bằng cách này, ca múa tập thể phát huy một sức mạnh kỳ diệu giúp đoàn kết các bộ tộc.
Khi nền văn minh nhân loại phát triển, ca múa tập thể có chức năng cao quý và phức tạp hơn nhiều, chẳng hạn như được sử dụng trong các nghi lễ tôn giáo hoặc thể hiện những cảm xúc tinh tế bên trong con người. Tuy nhiên, ca múa tập thể vẫn còn tồn tại trong các cuộc tuần hành và cổ vũ theo nhóm của các ban nhạc quân đội, thể hiện vai trò cơ bản mà nó từng đảm nhận trong những ngày đầu của lịch sử nhân loại.
g_text_translate.en:
In the course of the development of human civilization, the invention of group singing and dancing was a landmark event. The act of matching the beat by shouting together and moving the body rhythmically had a remarkable effect, producing strong cooperation and harmony within the group. In other words, by singing and dancing together during the festival, the participants felt a sense of emotional solidarity and resolved conflicts. Also, during the war, the warriors temporarily overcome the fear of death through group singing and dancing and gained courage to throw their whole body willingly to protect the whole group. In this way, group singing and dancing exerted a magical power that united tribes.
As human civilization developed, group singing and dancing took on a much more sophisticated and noble function, such as being used in religious ceremonies or expressing the delicate emotions within humans. Nevertheless, group singing and dancing still remain in marches and group cheers of military bands, demonstrating the basic role they played in the early days of human history.
g_text_translate.fr:
Au cours du développement de la civilisation humaine, l'invention du chant et de la danse de groupe a été un événement marquant. Le fait de faire correspondre le rythme en criant ensemble et en bougeant le corps en rythme a eu un effet remarquable, produisant une coopération et une harmonie fortes au sein du groupe. En d'autres termes, en chantant et en dansant ensemble pendant le festival, les participants ont ressenti un sentiment de solidarité émotionnelle et ont résolu des conflits. De plus, pendant la guerre, les guerriers ont temporairement surmonté la peur de la mort grâce au chant et à la danse de groupe et ont pris le courage de jeter volontairement tout leur corps pour protéger l'ensemble du groupe. De cette façon, le chant et la danse de groupe exerçaient un pouvoir magique qui unissait les tribus.
Au fur et à mesure que la civilisation humaine se développait, le chant et la danse de groupe ont pris une fonction beaucoup plus sophistiquée et noble, comme être utilisés dans les cérémonies religieuses ou exprimer les émotions délicates chez les humains. Néanmoins, le chant et la danse de groupe subsistent encore dans les marches et les acclamations de groupe des fanfares militaires, démontrant le rôle fondamental qu'ils ont joué dans les premiers jours de l'histoire humaine.

#######

In the course of the development of human civilization, the invention of group singing and dancing was a landmark event. The act of matching the beat by shouting together and moving the body rhythmically had a remarkable effect, producing strong cooperation and harmony within the group. In other words, by singing and dancing together during the festival, the participants felt a sense of emotional solidarity and resolved conflicts. Also, during the war, the warriors temporarily overcome the fear of death through group singing and dancing and gained courage to throw their whole body willingly to protect the whole group. In this way, group singing and dancing exerted a magical power that united tribes.
As human civilization developed, group singing and dancing took on a much more sophisticated and noble function, such as being used in religious ceremonies or expressing the delicate emotions within humans. Nevertheless, group singing and dancing still remain in marches and group cheers of military bands, demonstrating the basic role they played in the early days of human history.
g_text_translate.es:
En el curso del desarrollo de la civilización humana, la invención del canto y el baile en grupo fue un hito. El acto de igualar el ritmo gritando juntos y moviendo el cuerpo rítmicamente tuvo un efecto notable, produciendo una fuerte cooperación y armonía dentro del grupo. En otras palabras, al cantar y bailar juntos durante el festival, los participantes sintieron una sensación de solidaridad emocional y resolvieron conflictos. Además, durante la guerra, los guerreros superaron temporalmente el miedo a la muerte a través del canto y el baile en grupo y adquirieron valor para arrojar todo su cuerpo voluntariamente para proteger a todo el grupo. De esta manera, el canto y el baile en grupo ejercían un poder mágico que unía a las tribus.
A medida que se desarrolló la civilización humana, el canto y el baile en grupo adquirieron una función mucho más sofisticada y noble, como ser utilizados en ceremonias religiosas o expresar las delicadas emociones de los humanos. Sin embargo, el canto y el baile grupales aún permanecen en marchas y vítores grupales de bandas militares, lo que demuestra el papel básico que desempeñaron en los primeros días de la historia humana.

#######

Also, during the war, the warriors temporarily overcome the fear of death through group singing and dancing and gained courage to throw their whole body willingly to protect the whole group. In this way, group singing and dancing exerted a magical power that united tribes.
As human civilization developed, group singing and dancing took on a much more sophisticated and noble function, such as being used in religious ceremonies or expressing the delicate emotions within humans. Nevertheless, group singing and dancing still remain in marches and group cheers of military bands, demonstrating the basic role they played in the early days of human history.
g_text_translate.id:
Dalam perjalanan perkembangan peradaban manusia, penemuan nyanyian dan tarian kelompok merupakan peristiwa penting. Tindakan mencocokkan irama dengan berteriak bersama dan menggerakkan tubuh secara berirama memiliki efek yang luar biasa, menghasilkan kerjasama yang kuat dan harmoni dalam kelompok. Dengan kata lain, dengan bernyanyi dan menari bersama selama festival, para peserta merasakan solidaritas emosional dan menyelesaikan konflik. Juga, selama perang, para pejuang untuk sementara mengatasi ketakutan akan kematian melalui nyanyian dan tarian kelompok dan memperoleh keberanian untuk melemparkan seluruh tubuh mereka dengan sukarela untuk melindungi seluruh kelompok. Dengan cara ini, nyanyian dan tarian kelompok mengerahkan kekuatan magis yang menyatukan suku-suku.
Seiring berkembangnya peradaban manusia, nyanyian dan tarian kelompok mengambil fungsi yang jauh lebih canggih dan mulia, seperti digunakan dalam upacara keagamaan atau mengekspresikan emosi halus dalam diri manusia. Namun demikian, nyanyian dan tarian kelompok masih tetap ada dalam pawai dan sorak-sorai kelompok band-band militer, menunjukkan peran dasar yang mereka mainkan di masa-masa awal sejarah manusia.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
人類文明の発展の過程で、グループの歌と踊りの発明は画期的な出来事でした.一緒に叫び、リズミカルに体を動かすことでビートを合わせる行為は、グループ内の強い協力と調和を生み出す効果が顕著でした。つまり、祭りの期間中一緒に歌ったり踊ったりすることで、参加者は感情的な連帯感を感じ、対立を解決したのです。また、戦時中、武士たちは集団で歌ったり踊ったりすることで一時的に死の恐怖を克服し、集団全体を守るために進んで全身を投げる勇気を得ました。このように、集団で歌ったり踊ったりすることは、部族を結びつける魔法の力を発揮しました。
人類の文明が発展するにつれて、集団での歌や踊りは、宗教的な儀式で使用されたり、人間の繊細な感情を表現したりするなど、より洗練された高貴な機能を帯びるようになりました.それにもかかわらず、グループの歌と踊りはまだ軍楽隊の行進とグループの歓声に残っており、人類の歴史の初期に彼らが果たした基本的な役割を示しています.

#######

In the course of the development of human civilization, the invention of group singing and dancing was a landmark event. The act of matching the beat by shouting together and moving the body rhythmically had a remarkable effect, producing strong cooperation and harmony within the group. In other words, by singing and dancing together during the festival, the participants felt a sense of emotional solidarity and resolved conflicts. Also, during the war, the warriors temporarily overcome the fear of death through group singing and dancing and gained courage to throw their whole body willingly to protect the whole group. In this way, group singing and dancing exerted a magical power that united tribes.
As human civilization developed, group singing and dancing took on a much more sophisticated and noble function, such as being used in religious ceremonies or expressing the delicate emotions within humans. Nevertheless, group singing and dancing still remain in marches and group cheers of military bands, demonstrating the basic role they played in the early days of human history.
g_text_translate.de:
Im Laufe der Entwicklung der menschlichen Zivilisation war die Erfindung des gemeinsamen Singens und Tanzens ein Meilenstein. Der Akt des Anpassens des Beats durch gemeinsames Schreien und rhythmisches Bewegen des Körpers hatte eine bemerkenswerte Wirkung und erzeugte eine starke Zusammenarbeit und Harmonie innerhalb der Gruppe. Mit anderen Worten, durch das gemeinsame Singen und Tanzen während des Festivals empfanden die Teilnehmer ein Gefühl der emotionalen Solidarität und lösten Konflikte. Auch während des Krieges überwanden die Krieger vorübergehend die Angst vor dem Tod durch gemeinsames Singen und Tanzen und fassten den Mut, bereitwillig ihren ganzen Körper zu werfen, um die gesamte Gruppe zu schützen. Auf diese Weise übte das Singen und Tanzen in der Gruppe eine magische Kraft aus, die Stämme vereinte.
Als sich die menschliche Zivilisation entwickelte, übernahmen das Singen und Tanzen in Gruppen eine viel raffiniertere und edlere Funktion, wie z. B. die Verwendung in religiösen Zeremonien oder das Ausdrücken der empfindlichen Emotionen in Menschen. Dennoch bleiben Gruppengesang und -tanz in Märschen und Gruppenjubeln von Militärkapellen erhalten, was die grundlegende Rolle demonstriert, die sie in den frühen Tagen der Menschheitsgeschichte gespielt haben.

#######

In the course of the development of human civilization, the invention of group singing and dancing was a landmark event. The act of matching the beat by shouting together and moving the body rhythmically had a remarkable effect, producing strong cooperation and harmony within the group. In other words, by singing and dancing together during the festival, the participants felt a sense of emotional solidarity and resolved conflicts. Also, during the war, the warriors temporarily overcome the fear of death through group singing and dancing and gained courage to throw their whole body willingly to protect the whole group. In this way, group singing and dancing exerted a magical power that united tribes.
As human civilization developed, group singing and dancing took on a much more sophisticated and noble function, such as being used in religious ceremonies or expressing the delicate emotions within humans. Nevertheless, group singing and dancing still remain in marches and group cheers of military bands, demonstrating the basic role they played in the early days of human history.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
在人类文明的发展过程中，集体歌舞的发明是具有里程碑意义的事件。通过一起喊叫和有节奏地移动身体来匹配节拍的行为产生了显着的效果，在团队内部产生了强烈的合作与和谐。换句话说，通过在节日期间一起唱歌和跳舞，参与者感受到了情感上的团结并化解了冲突。此外，在战争期间，战士们通过集体唱歌和跳舞暂时克服了对死亡的恐惧，获得了自愿投掷全身保护整个集体的勇气。就这样，集体歌舞发挥了团结部落的神奇力量。
随着人类文明的发展，集体歌舞具有了更为复杂和高尚的功能，例如用于宗教仪式或表达人类内心的微妙情感。尽管如此，军乐队的进行曲和集体欢呼中仍保留着集体歌舞，显示了它们在人类历史早期发挥的基本作用。

g_text_translate.it:
Nel corso dello sviluppo della civiltà umana, l'invenzione del canto e della danza di gruppo è stata un evento fondamentale. L'atto di intonare il ritmo gridando insieme e muovendo ritmicamente il corpo ha avuto un notevole effetto, producendo una forte cooperazione e armonia all'interno del gruppo. In altre parole, cantando e ballando insieme durante il festival, i partecipanti hanno provato un senso di solidarietà emotiva e hanno risolto i conflitti. Inoltre, durante la guerra, i guerrieri superarono temporaneamente la paura della morte attraverso canti e balli di gruppo e trovarono il coraggio di gettare volontariamente tutto il loro corpo per proteggere l'intero gruppo. In questo modo, i canti e le danze di gruppo esercitavano un potere magico che univa le tribù.
Con lo sviluppo della civiltà umana, il canto e la danza di gruppo hanno assunto una funzione molto più sofisticata e nobile, come essere usati nelle cerimonie religiose o esprimere le delicate emozioni degli esseri umani. Tuttavia, il canto e la danza di gruppo rimangono ancora nelle marce e negli applausi di gruppo delle bande militari, a dimostrazione del ruolo fondamentale che hanno svolto nei primi giorni della storia umana.

g_text_translate.ru:
В ходе развития человеческой цивилизации эпохальным событием стало изобретение группового пения и танцев. Акт согласования ритма посредством совместного крика и ритмических движений тела имел замечательный эффект, создавая прочное сотрудничество и гармонию внутри группы. Другими словами, совместными песнями и танцами во время фестиваля участники ощутили чувство эмоциональной солидарности и разрешили конфликты. Кроме того, во время войны воины временно преодолели страх смерти посредством группового пения и танцев и набрались смелости добровольно бросить все свое тело, чтобы защитить всю группу. Таким образом, групповое пение и танцы оказывали магическую силу, объединявшую племена.
По мере развития человеческой цивилизации групповое пение и танцы приобрели гораздо более сложную и благородную функцию, например, использовались в религиозных церемониях или выражали тонкие эмоции внутри людей. Тем не менее, групповое пение и танцы по-прежнему присутствуют в маршах и групповых аплодисментах военных оркестров, демонстрируя основную роль, которую они играли на заре человеческой истории.

g_text_translate.th:
ในระหว่างการพัฒนาอารยธรรมของมนุษย์ การประดิษฐ์การร้องเพลงและเต้นรำเป็นกลุ่มถือเป็นเหตุการณ์สำคัญ การจับคู่จังหวะด้วยการตะโกนพร้อมกันและขยับร่างกายเป็นจังหวะมีผลอย่างน่าทึ่ง ทำให้เกิดความร่วมมือและความสามัคคีที่เข้มแข็งภายในกลุ่ม กล่าวอีกนัยหนึ่ง โดยการร้องเพลงและเต้นรำร่วมกันในช่วงเทศกาล ผู้เข้าร่วมรู้สึกถึงความสามัคคีทางอารมณ์และแก้ไขข้อขัดแย้งได้ นอกจากนี้ในช่วงสงคราม นักรบเอาชนะความกลัวความตายได้ชั่วคราวด้วยการร้องเพลงและเต้นรำเป็นกลุ่ม และได้รับความกล้าที่จะทุ่มทั้งตัวด้วยความเต็มใจที่จะปกป้องทั้งกลุ่ม ด้วยวิธีนี้ การร้องเพลงและเต้นรำเป็นกลุ่มจึงทำให้เกิดพลังเวทย์มนตร์ที่รวมชนเผ่าเข้าด้วยกัน
เมื่ออารยธรรมของมนุษย์พัฒนาขึ้น การร้องเพลงและเต้นรำเป็นกลุ่มก็มีบทบาทที่ซับซ้อนและมีเกียรติมากขึ้น เช่น ใช้ในพิธีทางศาสนาหรือแสดงอารมณ์อันละเอียดอ่อนภายในมนุษย์ อย่างไรก็ตาม การร้องเพลงและเต้นรำเป็นกลุ่มยังคงอยู่ในการเดินขบวนและเสียงเชียร์ของกลุ่มทหาร แสดงให้เห็นบทบาทพื้นฐานที่พวกเขาเล่นในสมัยแรกเริ่มของประวัติศาสตร์มนุษยชาติ

g_text_translate.pt:
No curso do desenvolvimento da civilização humana, a invenção do canto e da dança em grupo foi um acontecimento marcante. O ato de combinar a batida gritando juntos e movendo o corpo ritmicamente teve um efeito notável, produzindo forte cooperação e harmonia dentro do grupo. Ou seja, ao cantar e dançar juntos durante o festival, os participantes sentiram solidariedade emocional e resolveram conflitos. Além disso, durante a guerra, os guerreiros superaram temporariamente o medo da morte por meio de cantos e danças em grupo e ganharam coragem para lançar todo o corpo de boa vontade para proteger todo o grupo. Dessa forma, o canto e a dança em grupo exerciam um poder mágico que unia as tribos.
À medida que a civilização humana se desenvolveu, o canto e a dança em grupo assumiram uma função muito mais sofisticada e nobre, como ser usado em cerimónias religiosas ou expressar as emoções delicadas dos humanos. No entanto, o canto e a dança em grupo ainda permanecem nas marchas e nas comemorações das bandas militares, demonstrando o papel básico que desempenharam nos primeiros dias da história humana.
