general.g_text: 옛날 한국인들은 공간 안팎에 대한 인식이 현재와 상이했다. 그래서 현대의 정원과 같은 인공적인 자연 공간을 가옥의 울타리 안에 조성하지 않았다. 왜냐하면 공간을 내부와 외부로 명확히 경계를 지어 구분하지 않았기 때문이다. 일반 서민들의 가옥은 담장이 없거나 매우 낮았고 개폐가 용이한 사립문으로 그저 상징적으로 공간을 구획했을 뿐이다. 문을 개방하면 산천초목이 시야에 들어오는 환경에서 그것을 인공적으로 축소해 담장 내부로 끌어들일 필요가 없었다. 자연을 (        ) 했던 것이다. 양반 가옥의 ‘대청마루’를 봐도 이 같은 가치관을 확인할 수 있다.  현대의 거실이 실외와 공간적으로 완전히 분리되어 있는 것과 달리 전통 가옥의 ‘대청마루’는 외부와 차단되어 있지 않다. 옛날 한국인들은 집안에서 자연을 마음껏 즐기며 생활했던 것이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ngày xưa, người Hàn Quốc nhận thức về không gian bên trong và bên ngoài khác với hiện tại. Vì vậy, không gian thiên nhiên mang tính nhân tạo như vườn hiện đại đã không được xây dựng bên trong hàng rào nhà. Đó là bởi vì không có sự phân biệt rõ ràng giữa không gian bên trong và bên ngoài. Ngôi nhà của những người dân bình thường không có hàng rào hoặc có nhưng rất thấp, và người ta chỉ chia không gian một cách tượng trưng bằng những cánh cổng tre dễ đóng và mở. Trong môi trường có thể thu vào mắt cây cối núi non sông suối khi vừa mở cửa ra, không có lý do gì khiến người ta phải thu nhỏ chúng một cách nhân tạo và kéo chúng về sau bờ rào cả. Đó là việc (        ) thiên nhiên. Nếu nhìn vào 'sàn hiên trước' của nhà quý tộc thì cũng có thể xác nhận được giá trị quan đó. Khác với phòng khách hiện đại tách biệt hoàn toàn với không gian bên ngoài, 'sàn hiên trước' của nhà truyền thống không bị ngăn cách với bên ngoài. Ngày xưa người Hàn Quốc sống và tận hưởng thiên nhiên thỏa thích trong nhà của họ.
g_text_translate.en: In the past, Koreans had a different perception of the inside and outside of space than they do today. Therefore, artificial natural space like a modern garden was not created within the fence of the house. This is because the space was not clearly demarcated between the inside and the outside. The houses of ordinary people had no walls or were very low, and they only symbolically divided the space with private doors that were easy to open and close. In an environment where the mountains and streams can be seen just by opening the door, there is no need to artificially reduce it and drag it into the wall. People tried to leave nature as it is and enjoy it as much as possible. Such values ​​can be confirmed by looking at the 'Daecheongmaru' of the nobleman's house. Unlike the modern living room, which is spatially separated from the outdoors, the 'daecheongmaru' of the traditional house is not blocked from the outside. In the old days, Koreans lived in their homes, enjoying nature to the fullest.
g_text_translate.fr:
Dans le passé, les Coréens avaient une perception différente de l'intérieur et de l'extérieur de l'espace qu'aujourd'hui. Par conséquent, un espace naturel artificiel comme un jardin moderne n'a pas été créé à l'intérieur de la clôture de la maison. C'est parce que l'espace n'était pas clairement délimité entre l'intérieur et l'extérieur. Les maisons des gens ordinaires n'avaient pas de murs ou étaient très basses, et elles ne divisaient symboliquement l'espace que par des portes privées faciles à ouvrir et à fermer. Dans un environnement où les montagnes et les ruisseaux peuvent être vus simplement en ouvrant la porte, il n'est pas nécessaire de la réduire artificiellement et de la faire glisser dans le mur. Les gens ont essayé de laisser la nature telle qu'elle est et d'en profiter le plus possible. De telles valeurs peuvent être confirmées en regardant le 'Daecheongmaru' de la maison du noble. Contrairement au salon moderne, qui est spatialement séparé de l'extérieur, le « daecheongmaru » de la maison traditionnelle n'est pas bloqué de l'extérieur. Autrefois, les Coréens vivaient dans leurs maisons, profitant pleinement de la nature.

#######

In the past, Koreans had a different perception of the inside and outside of space than they do today. Therefore, artificial natural space like a modern garden was not created within the fence of the house. This is because the space was not clearly demarcated between the inside and the outside. The houses of ordinary people had no walls or were very low, and they only symbolically divided the space with private doors that were easy to open and close. In an environment where the mountains and streams can be seen just by opening the door, there is no need to artificially reduce it and drag it into the wall. People tried to leave nature as it is and enjoy it as much as possible. Such values ​​can be confirmed by looking at the 'Daecheongmaru' of the nobleman's house. Unlike the modern living room, which is spatially separated from the outdoors, the 'daecheongmaru' of the traditional house is not blocked from the outside. In the old days, Koreans lived in their homes, enjoying nature to the fullest.
g_text_translate.es:
En el pasado, los coreanos tenían una percepción diferente del interior y el exterior del espacio que la que tienen hoy. Por lo tanto, no se creó un espacio natural artificial como un jardín moderno dentro de la cerca de la casa. Esto se debe a que el espacio no estaba claramente delimitado entre el interior y el exterior. Las casas de la gente común no tenían paredes o eran muy bajas, y solo dividían simbólicamente el espacio con puertas privadas que eran fáciles de abrir y cerrar. En un entorno donde las montañas y los arroyos se pueden ver con solo abrir la puerta, no hay necesidad de reducirla artificialmente y arrastrarla hacia la pared. La gente trató de dejar la naturaleza como está y disfrutarla tanto como fuera posible. Dichos valores pueden confirmarse mirando el 'Daecheongmaru' de la casa del noble. A diferencia de la sala de estar moderna, que está espacialmente separada del exterior, el 'daecheongmaru' de la casa tradicional no está bloqueado desde el exterior. En los viejos tiempos, los coreanos vivían en sus casas, disfrutando de la naturaleza al máximo.

#######

In the past, Koreans had a different perception of the inside and outside of space than they do today. Therefore, artificial natural space like a modern garden was not created within the fence of the house. This is because the space was not clearly demarcated between the inside and the outside. The houses of ordinary people had no walls or were very low, and they only symbolically divided the space with private doors that were easy to open and close. In an environment where the mountains and streams can be seen just by opening the door, there is no need to artificially reduce it and drag it into the wall. People tried to leave nature as it is and enjoy it as much as possible. Such values ​​can be confirmed by looking at the 'Daecheongmaru' of the nobleman's house. Unlike the modern living room, which is spatially separated from the outdoors, the 'daecheongmaru' of the traditional house is not blocked from the outside. In the old days, Koreans lived in their homes, enjoying nature to the fullest.
g_text_translate.id:
Di masa lalu, orang Korea memiliki persepsi yang berbeda tentang ruang dalam dan luar dari yang mereka lakukan saat ini. Oleh karena itu, ruang alami buatan seperti taman modern tidak dibuat di dalam pagar rumah. Hal ini karena ruang tidak jelas batas antara bagian dalam dan luar. Rumah-rumah orang biasa tidak berdinding atau sangat rendah, dan mereka hanya secara simbolis membagi ruang dengan pintu-pintu pribadi yang mudah dibuka dan ditutup. Di lingkungan di mana gunung dan sungai dapat dilihat hanya dengan membuka pintu, tidak perlu menguranginya secara artifisial dan menyeretnya ke dinding. Orang-orang berusaha meninggalkan alam apa adanya dan menikmatinya semaksimal mungkin. Nilai-nilai seperti itu dapat dikonfirmasi dengan melihat 'Daecheongmaru' dari rumah bangsawan. Berbeda dengan ruang tamu modern yang secara spasial terpisah dari luar, 'daecheongmaru' rumah tradisional tidak terhalang dari luar. Di masa lalu, orang Korea tinggal di rumah mereka, menikmati alam sepenuhnya.

#######

In the past, Koreans had a different perception of the inside and outside of space than they do today. Therefore, artificial natural space like a modern garden was not created within the fence of the house. This is because the space was not clearly demarcated between the inside and the outside. The houses of ordinary people had no walls or were very low, and they only symbolically divided the space with private doors that were easy to open and close. In an environment where the mountains and streams can be seen just by opening the door, there is no need to artificially reduce it and drag it into the wall. People tried to leave nature as it is and enjoy it as much as possible. Such values ​​can be confirmed by looking at the 'Daecheongmaru' of the nobleman's house. Unlike the modern living room, which is spatially separated from the outdoors, the 'daecheongmaru' of the traditional house is not blocked from the outside. In the old days, Koreans lived in their homes, enjoying nature to the fullest.
g_text_translate.ja:
過去の韓国人は、現在とは異なる空間の内側と外側の認識を持っていました。そのため、家の塀の中に現代の庭のような人工的な自然空間は作られませんでした。これは、空間の内と外が明確に区切られていなかったためです。庶民の家は壁がなく、非常に低く、開閉しやすい専用の扉で空間を象徴的に仕切っているだけでした。ドアを開けるだけで山や川が見える環境で、人為的に縮小して壁に引きずり込む必要はありません。人々は自然をあるがままに残して、できるだけ楽しむように努めてきました。このような価値観は、貴族の家の「大清丸」を見れば確認できます。屋外と空間的に分離されたモダンなリビングルームとは異なり、伝統家屋の「大清丸」は屋外から遮断されていません。昔、韓国人は家に住み、自然を満喫していました。

#######

In the past, Koreans had a different perception of the inside and outside of space than they do today. Therefore, artificial natural space like a modern garden was not created within the fence of the house. This is because the space was not clearly demarcated between the inside and the outside. The houses of ordinary people had no walls or were very low, and they only symbolically divided the space with private doors that were easy to open and close. In an environment where the mountains and streams can be seen just by opening the door, there is no need to artificially reduce it and drag it into the wall. People tried to leave nature as it is and enjoy it as much as possible. Such values ​​can be confirmed by looking at the 'Daecheongmaru' of the nobleman's house. Unlike the modern living room, which is spatially separated from the outdoors, the 'daecheongmaru' of the traditional house is not blocked from the outside. In the old days, Koreans lived in their homes, enjoying nature to the fullest.
g_text_translate.de:
Früher hatten die Koreaner eine andere Wahrnehmung vom Innen und Außen des Weltraums als heute. Daher wurde innerhalb des Zauns des Hauses kein künstlicher Naturraum wie ein moderner Garten geschaffen. Denn der Raum war nicht klar zwischen Innen und Außen abgegrenzt. Die Häuser der einfachen Leute hatten keine Wände oder waren sehr niedrig und teilten den Raum nur symbolisch mit privaten Türen, die sich leicht öffnen und schließen ließen. In einer Umgebung, in der die Berge und Bäche nur durch das Öffnen der Tür zu sehen sind, besteht keine Notwendigkeit, sie künstlich zu reduzieren und in die Wand zu ziehen. Die Menschen versuchten, die Natur so zu lassen, wie sie ist, und sie so gut wie möglich zu genießen. Solche Werte können durch einen Blick auf die 'Daecheongmaru' des Adelshauses bestätigt werden. Im Gegensatz zum modernen Wohnzimmer, das räumlich von der Außenwelt getrennt ist, ist das „Daecheongmaru“ des traditionellen Hauses nicht von außen blockiert. Früher lebten die Koreaner in ihren Häusern und genossen die Natur in vollen Zügen.

#######

In the past, Koreans had a different perception of the inside and outside of space than they do today. Therefore, artificial natural space like a modern garden was not created within the fence of the house. This is because the space was not clearly demarcated between the inside and the outside. The houses of ordinary people had no walls or were very low, and they only symbolically divided the space with private doors that were easy to open and close. In an environment where the mountains and streams can be seen just by opening the door, there is no need to artificially reduce it and drag it into the wall. People tried to leave nature as it is and enjoy it as much as possible. Such values ​​can be confirmed by looking at the 'Daecheongmaru' of the nobleman's house. Unlike the modern living room, which is spatially separated from the outdoors, the 'daecheongmaru' of the traditional house is not blocked from the outside. In the old days, Koreans lived in their homes, enjoying nature to the fullest.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
过去，韩国人对空间内部和外部的看法与今天不同。因此，像现代花园这样的人工自然空间并没有在房子的围墙内创造出来。这是因为内部和外部之间的空间没有明确划分。普通人的房子没有围墙或很低，只是象征性地用易于开启和关闭的私人门来划分空间。在开门就能看到山川溪流的环境中，不需要人为地缩小，拖入墙内。人们试图离开自然，尽可能地享受它。这样的价值观可以通过看贵族家的“大清丸”来证实。与在空间上与室外隔开的现代客厅不同，传统住宅的“大青丸”不受外界阻挡。过去，韩国人住在家里，尽情享受大自然。

Unlike the modern living room, which is spatially separated from the outdoors, the 'daecheongmaru' of the traditional house is not blocked from the outside. In the old days, Koreans lived in their homes, enjoying nature to the fullest.

過去，韓國人對空間內部和外部的看法與今天不同。因此，像現代花園這樣的人工自然空間並沒有在房子的圍牆內創造出來。這是因為內部和外部之間的空間沒有明確劃分。普通人的房子沒有圍牆或很低，只是像徵性地用易於開啟和關閉的私人門來劃分空間。在開門就能看到山川溪流的環境中，不需要人為地縮小，拖入牆內。人們試圖離開自然，盡可能地享受它。這樣的價值觀可以通過看貴族家的“大清丸”來證實。與在空間上與室外隔開的現代客廳不同，傳統住宅的“大青丸”不受外界阻擋。過去，韓國人住在家裡，盡情享受大自然。

g_text_translate.it:
In passato, i coreani avevano una percezione diversa dell'interno e dell'esterno dello spazio rispetto a quella che hanno oggi. Pertanto, all'interno del recinto della casa non è stato creato uno spazio naturale artificiale come un giardino moderno. Questo perché lo spazio non era chiaramente delimitato tra il dentro e il fuori. Le case della gente comune non avevano muri o erano molto basse, e dividevano solo simbolicamente lo spazio con porte private facili da aprire e chiudere. In un ambiente in cui le montagne e i ruscelli possono essere visti semplicemente aprendo la porta, non è necessario ridurlo artificialmente e trascinarlo nel muro. Le persone hanno cercato di lasciare la natura così com'è e godersela il più possibile. Tali valori possono essere confermati osservando il 'Daecheongmaru' della casa del nobile. A differenza del soggiorno moderno, che è spazialmente separato dall'esterno, il 'daecheongmaru' della casa tradizionale non è bloccato dall'esterno. Ai vecchi tempi, i coreani vivevano nelle loro case, godendosi al massimo la natura.

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
В прошлом у корейцев было другое восприятие внутреннего и внешнего пространства, чем сегодня. Поэтому искусственное природное пространство типа современного сада внутри ограды дома не создавалось. Это связано с тем, что пространство не было четко разграничено между внутренней и внешней частью. Дома простых людей не имели стен или были очень низкими, а лишь символически делили пространство частными дверями, которые легко открывались и закрывались. В среде, где горы и ручьи можно увидеть, просто открыв дверь, нет необходимости искусственно уменьшать ее и перетаскивать в стену. Люди старались оставить природу такой, какая она есть, и наслаждаться ею как можно больше. Такие ценности можно подтвердить, взглянув на «Тэчхонмару» дома дворянина. В отличие от современной гостиной, которая пространственно отделена от внешней среды, «тэчхонмару» традиционного дома не закрыта снаружи. В былые времена корейцы жили в своих домах, в полной мере наслаждаясь природой.

#######

g_text_translate.th:
ในอดีต ชาวเกาหลีมีการรับรู้ภายในและภายนอกพื้นที่ที่แตกต่างจากในปัจจุบัน ดังนั้นจึงไม่ได้สร้างพื้นที่ธรรมชาติเทียมแบบสวนสมัยใหม่ไว้ภายในรั้วบ้าน เนื่องจากพื้นที่ไม่ได้แบ่งเขตระหว่างด้านในและด้านนอกอย่างชัดเจน บ้านของคนธรรมดาไม่มีกำแพงหรืออยู่ต่ำมาก และมีเพียงการแบ่งพื้นที่ในเชิงสัญลักษณ์ด้วยประตูส่วนตัวที่เปิดและปิดได้ง่าย ในสภาพแวดล้อมที่สามารถมองเห็นภูเขาและลำธารเพียงแค่เปิดประตู ก็ไม่จำเป็นต้องลดขนาดลงและลากเข้าไปในผนัง ผู้คนพยายามละทิ้งธรรมชาติอย่างที่มันเป็นและสนุกกับมันให้มากที่สุด ค่านิยมดังกล่าวสามารถยืนยันได้โดยการดูที่ 'แดชองมารู' ของบ้านขุนนาง ต่างจากห้องนั่งเล่นสมัยใหม่ซึ่งแยกพื้นที่ออกจากภายนอก 'แดชองมารู' ของบ้านแบบดั้งเดิมไม่ได้ถูกปิดกั้นจากภายนอก ในสมัยก่อน ชาวเกาหลีอาศัยอยู่ในบ้านและเพลิดเพลินกับธรรมชาติอย่างเต็มที่

g_text_translate.pt:
No passado, os coreanos tinham uma percepção do interior e do exterior do espaço diferente da que têm hoje. Portanto, um espaço natural artificial como um jardim moderno não foi criado dentro da cerca da casa. Isso porque o espaço não estava claramente demarcado entre o interior e o exterior. As casas das pessoas comuns não tinham paredes ou eram muito baixas, e apenas dividiam simbolicamente o espaço com portas privadas fáceis de abrir e fechar. Num ambiente onde as montanhas e riachos podem ser vistos apenas abrindo a porta, não há necessidade de reduzi-los artificialmente e arrastá-los para a parede. As pessoas tentaram deixar a natureza como ela é e aproveitá-la tanto quanto possível. Tais valores podem ser confirmados observando o ‘Daecheongmaru’ da casa do nobre. Ao contrário da sala de estar moderna, que está espacialmente separada do exterior, o 'daecheongmaru' da casa tradicional não é bloqueado pelo exterior. Antigamente, os coreanos viviam em suas casas, aproveitando ao máximo a natureza.

g_text_translate.hi:
अतीत में, कोरियाई लोगों की अंतरिक्ष के अंदर और बाहर के बारे में आज की तुलना में अलग धारणा थी। इसलिए, घर की बाड़ के भीतर आधुनिक उद्यान जैसी कृत्रिम प्राकृतिक जगह नहीं बनाई गई। ऐसा इसलिए है क्योंकि अंदर और बाहर के बीच जगह स्पष्ट रूप से सीमांकित नहीं थी। सामान्य लोगों के घरों में कोई दीवार नहीं होती थी या बहुत नीची होती थी, और वे केवल प्रतीकात्मक रूप से उस स्थान को निजी दरवाजों से विभाजित करते थे जिन्हें खोलना और बंद करना आसान होता था। ऐसे माहौल में जहां सिर्फ दरवाजा खोलने से पहाड़ और झरने दिखाई देते हों, वहां इसे कृत्रिम रूप से छोटा करके दीवार में खींचने की कोई जरूरत नहीं है। लोगों ने प्रकृति को वैसे ही छोड़ने और जितना संभव हो सके इसका आनंद लेने की कोशिश की। ऐसे मूल्यों की पुष्टि रईस के घर के 'डेचेओंगमारू' को देखकर की जा सकती है। आधुनिक लिविंग रूम के विपरीत, जो स्थानिक रूप से बाहर से अलग होता है, पारंपरिक घर का 'डेचेओंगमारू' बाहर से अवरुद्ध नहीं होता है। पुराने दिनों में, कोरियाई लोग अपने घरों में रहते थे और प्रकृति का भरपूर आनंद लेते थे।

#######

g_text_translate.tr:
Geçmişte Korelilerin uzayın içi ve dışı ile ilgili algıları bugün olduğundan farklıydı. Bu nedenle evin çiti içerisinde modern bir bahçe gibi yapay doğal alan oluşturulmadı. Bunun nedeni, içerisi ile dışarısı arasındaki alanın net bir şekilde sınırlandırılmamasıdır. Sıradan insanların evlerinin duvarları yoktu ya da çok alçaktı ve alanı yalnızca sembolik olarak kolay açılıp kapanabilen özel kapılarla bölüyordu. Sadece kapı açıldığında dağların ve derelerin görülebildiği bir ortamda, onu yapay olarak küçültüp duvara sürüklemeye gerek yoktur. İnsanlar doğayı olduğu gibi bırakıp, mümkün olduğu kadar tadını çıkarmaya çalıştı. Bu tür değerler asilzadenin evinin 'Daecheongmaru'suna bakılarak doğrulanabilir. Mekansal olarak dış mekandan ayrılan modern oturma odasının aksine, geleneksel evin 'daecheongmaru'su dışarıdan engellenmiyor. Eski günlerde Koreliler evlerinde yaşıyor, doğanın tadını doyasıya çıkarıyorlardı.

#######

4. แนวคิดเรื่องอวกาศและทัศนคติของเกาหลีต่อธรรมชาติ

content.q_text: 이 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 현대 거실은 ‘대청마루’와 구조와 기능이 유사하다.
‘대청마루’는 집 안팎을 구분해 주는 문의 역할도 했다.
사립문은 쉽게 여닫을 수 있어 외부와의 차단성이 낮았다.
옛날에 한국에서는 마당에 정원을 만들어 자연을 감상했다.

explain.vi:
Chọn câu giống với nội dung của đoạn văn.
1. Phòng khách hiện đại có cấu trúc và chức năng tương tự như 'sàn hiên trước'. 
2. 'Sàn hiên trước' cũng đóng vai trò là cánh cửa phân biệt trong và ngoài nhà. 
3. Cổng tre có thể dễ dàng mở và đóng nên khả năng ngăn chặn với bên ngoài thấp. 
4. Ngày xưa ở Hàn Quốc, người ta làm ra một khu vườn trong sân và thưởng thức thiên nhiên.
--------------------
Những cánh cổng tre dễ đóng và dễ mở, tức là có khả năng ngăn chặn với bên ngoài thấp. 3 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the one that is similar to the content of the paragraph.
1. The modern living room is similar in structure and function to ‘daecheongmaru’.
2. ‘Daecheongmaru’ also served as a door separating the inside and outside of the house.
3. The private door can be easily opened and closed, so the barrier to the outside is low.
4. In the old days, in Korea, people made a garden in the yard to appreciate nature.
--------------------
Bamboo gates are easy to close and open, meaning they have a low barrier to the outside. 3 is the correct answer.
explain.fr:
Choisissez celui qui est similaire au contenu du paragraphe.
1. Le salon moderne a une structure et une fonction similaires à « daecheongmaru ».
2. « Daecheongmaru » servait également de porte séparant l'intérieur et l'extérieur de la maison.
3. La porte privée peut être facilement ouverte et fermée, de sorte que la barrière vers l'extérieur est basse.
4. Autrefois, en Corée, les gens faisaient un jardin dans la cour pour apprécier la nature.
--------------------
Les portails en bambou sont faciles à fermer et à ouvrir, ce qui signifie qu'ils ont une faible barrière vers l'extérieur. 3 est la bonne réponse.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án đúng để điền vào phần (        ).
1. Cố gắng làm cho thăng hoa và thể hiện một cách nghệ thuật
2. Cố gắng nếu có thể thì để nguyên như vậy và tận hưởng
3. Cố gắng tránh sử dụng cho các mục đích mang tính sản xuất
4. Cố gắng một cách tích cực để bảo vệ và truyền lại cho thế hệ mai sau 
--------------------
Người ta không thu nhỏ thiên nhiên và kéo chúng về sau bờ rào, tức là cố gắng nếu có thể thì để nguyên như vậy và tận hưởng thiên nhiên. 2 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the correct answer to fill in (        ).
1. Try to express it by sublimating it artistically
2. Tried to leave it as it is and enjoy it as much as possible.
3. Try to not use it for productive purposes
4. Try to actively protect it and pass it on to future generations
--------------------

general.g_text: 동물은 물론 꽃의 세계에서도 이런 점을 찾아볼 수 있다. 다만 동물이 자신의 영역을 표시하기 위해 냄새를 사용하는 것에 반해 꽃은 번식을 위한 유인 수단으로 냄새를 풍긴다는 것이 다르다. 움직일 수 없는 꽃은 벌과 나비, 벌레 등의 도움을 받아야만 번식을 할 수 있다. 따라서 (        ) 유인하기 위한 도구로 꽃마다 각기 다른 독특한 냄새를 내뿜는다. 예를 들어 벌이나 나비를 유인해야 하는 꽃은 달콤한 향을 내고 벌레를 유인하려는 꽃은 과일향이나 곰팡이 냄새를 풍긴다. 유혹하고자 하는 대상이 좋아하는 냄새를 풍겨서 그들을 유인하려는 것이다. 또한 유인하려는 대상의 습성에 따라서 냄새를 풍기는 방법을 달리하기도 한다. 밤에 활동하는 나방은 시각 정보로는 위치를 찾을 수 없다. 그래서 나방을 유혹해야 하는 꽃은 다른 꽃들보다 냄새가 더 멀리까지 가도록 발산하게 된다.

g_text_translate.vi: Điều này có thể được tìm thấy không chỉ ở động vật, mà còn ở thế giới của các loài hoa. Sự khác biệt duy nhất là trong khi động vật sử dụng mùi hương để đánh dấu lãnh thổ của chúng, thì hoa phát ra mùi hương như một phương tiện thu hút để sinh sản. Một bông hoa không thể di chuyển chỉ có thể sinh sản với sự trợ giúp của ong, bướm, côn trùng,... Vì vậy, mỗi bông hoa tỏa ra một mùi đặc trưng khác nhau như một công cụ để thu hút (        ). Ví dụ, bông hoa cần thu hút ong hoặc bướm thì phải có mùi ngọt, và bông hoa muốn thu hút côn trùng thì phải phát ra mùi như trái cây hoặc nấm mốc. Đó là nỗ lực để thu hút đối tượng mà chúng muốn thu hút bằng cách tỏa ra mùi hương mà đối tượng thích. Ngoài ra, tùy thuộc vào tập tính của đối tượng muốn thu hút mà phương pháp phát ra mùi khác nhau. Loài bướm đêm hoạt động vào ban đêm không thể xác định được vị trí bằng thông tin trực quan. Vì vậy, những loài hoa phải dụ dỗ bướm đêm thì tỏa ra mùi hương bay xa hơn những loài hoa khác.
g_text_translate.en: This can be found not only in animals but also in the world of flowers. The only difference is that while animals use scent to mark their territory, flowers emit scent as an attracting means for reproduction. A flower that cannot move can only reproduce with the help of bees, butterflies, and insects. Therefore, each flower emits a unique odor as a tool to attract other creatures to help reproduce. For example, a flower that needs to attract bees or butterflies smells sweet, and a flower that wants to attract insects smells like fruit or mold. It is intended to lure the object to be seduced by giving off a pleasing scent. Also, depending on the habits of the target to be attracted, the method of exuding the smell is different. Moths that are active at night cannot be located using visual information. So, flowers that are supposed to attract moths emit a scent that travels farther than other flowers.
g_text_translate.fr:
Cela peut être trouvé non seulement chez les animaux mais aussi dans le monde des fleurs. La seule différence est que si les animaux utilisent l'odeur pour marquer leur territoire, les fleurs émettent une odeur comme moyen d'attraction pour la reproduction. Une fleur qui ne peut pas bouger ne peut se reproduire qu'avec l'aide d'abeilles, de papillons et d'insectes. Par conséquent, chaque fleur émet une odeur unique comme outil pour attirer d'autres créatures pour aider à se reproduire. Par exemple, une fleur qui doit attirer les abeilles ou les papillons sent bon, et une fleur qui veut attirer les insectes sent le fruit ou la moisissure. Il est destiné à attirer l'objet à séduire en dégageant un parfum agréable. Aussi, selon les habitudes de la cible à attirer, la méthode d'exsudation de l'odeur est différente. Les papillons qui sont actifs la nuit ne peuvent pas être localisés à l'aide d'informations visuelles. Ainsi, les fleurs censées attirer les mites émettent un parfum qui voyage plus loin que les autres fleurs.

#######

This can be found not only in animals but also in the world of flowers. The only difference is that while animals use scent to mark their territory, flowers emit scent as an attracting means for reproduction. A flower that cannot move can only reproduce with the help of bees, butterflies, and insects. Therefore, each flower emits a unique odor as a tool to attract other creatures to help reproduce. For example, a flower that needs to attract bees or butterflies smells sweet, and a flower that wants to attract insects smells like fruit or mold. It is intended to lure the object to be seduced by giving off a pleasing scent. Also, depending on the habits of the target to be attracted, the method of exuding the smell is different. Moths that are active at night cannot be located using visual information. So, flowers that are supposed to attract moths emit a scent that travels farther than other flowers.
g_text_translate.es:
Esto se puede encontrar no solo en los animales sino también en el mundo de las flores. La única diferencia es que mientras los animales usan el olor para marcar su territorio, las flores emiten olor como un medio de atracción para la reproducción. Una flor que no puede moverse solo puede reproducirse con la ayuda de abejas, mariposas e insectos. Por lo tanto, cada flor emite un olor único como herramienta para atraer a otras criaturas para ayudar a reproducirse. Por ejemplo, una flor que necesita atraer abejas o mariposas huele dulce, y una flor que quiere atraer insectos huele a fruta o moho. Se pretende atraer al objeto a seducir despidiendo un olor agradable. Además, dependiendo de los hábitos del objetivo a atraer, el método de exudar el olor es diferente. Las polillas que están activas durante la noche no se pueden localizar usando información visual. Entonces, las flores que se supone que atraen polillas emiten un aroma que viaja más lejos que otras flores.

#######

This can be found not only in animals but also in the world of flowers. The only difference is that while animals use scent to mark their territory, flowers emit scent as an attracting means for reproduction. A flower that cannot move can only reproduce with the help of bees, butterflies, and insects. Therefore, each flower emits a unique odor as a tool to attract other creatures to help reproduce. For example, a flower that needs to attract bees or butterflies smells sweet, and a flower that wants to attract insects smells like fruit or mold. It is intended to lure the object to be seduced by giving off a pleasing scent. Also, depending on the habits of the target to be attracted, the method of exuding the smell is different. Moths that are active at night cannot be located using visual information. So, flowers that are supposed to attract moths emit a scent that travels farther than other flowers.
g_text_translate.id:
Ini dapat ditemukan tidak hanya pada hewan tetapi juga di dunia bunga. Satu-satunya perbedaan adalah bahwa sementara hewan menggunakan aroma untuk menandai wilayah mereka, bunga memancarkan aroma sebagai sarana yang menarik untuk reproduksi. Bunga yang tidak dapat bergerak hanya dapat berkembang biak dengan bantuan lebah, kupu-kupu, dan serangga. Oleh karena itu, setiap bunga mengeluarkan bau yang unik sebagai alat untuk menarik makhluk lain untuk membantu berkembang biak. Misalnya, bunga yang ingin menarik lebah atau kupu-kupu berbau manis, dan bunga yang ingin menarik serangga berbau seperti buah atau jamur. Hal ini dimaksudkan untuk memikat objek yang akan dirayu dengan mengeluarkan aroma yang harum. Juga, tergantung pada kebiasaan target yang akan tertarik, metode memancarkan baunya berbeda. Ngengat yang aktif di malam hari tidak dapat ditemukan menggunakan informasi visual. Jadi, bunga yang seharusnya menarik ngengat mengeluarkan aroma yang menyebar lebih jauh dari bunga lainnya.

#######

This can be found not only in animals but also in the world of flowers. The only difference is that while animals use scent to mark their territory, flowers emit scent as an attracting means for reproduction. A flower that cannot move can only reproduce with the help of bees, butterflies, and insects. Therefore, each flower emits a unique odor as a tool to attract other creatures to help reproduce. For example, a flower that needs to attract bees or butterflies smells sweet, and a flower that wants to attract insects smells like fruit or mold. It is intended to lure the object to be seduced by giving off a pleasing scent. Also, depending on the habits of the target to be attracted, the method of exuding the smell is different. Moths that are active at night cannot be located using visual information. So, flowers that are supposed to attract moths emit a scent that travels farther than other flowers.
g_text_translate.ja:
これは動物だけでなく、花の世界にも見られます。唯一の違いは、動物が自分の縄張りを示すために匂いを使うのに対し、花は繁殖のための誘引手段として匂いを放つということです.動けない花は、ミツバチ、蝶、昆虫の助けを借りてのみ繁殖することができます.したがって、それぞれの花は、他の生き物を引き寄せて繁殖を助けるためのツールとして、独特の匂いを放ちます。たとえば、蜂や蝶を引き付ける必要がある花は甘い香りがし、昆虫を引き付けたい花は果物やカビのようなにおいがします。心地よい香りを放ち、誘惑対象をおびき寄せることを目的としています。また、誘う対象の習性によって、匂いの出し方も異なります。夜に活動する蛾は、視覚情報では位置を特定できません。そのため、蛾を引き寄せるはずの花は、他の花よりも遠くまで香りを放ちます。

#######

This can be found not only in animals but also in the world of flowers. The only difference is that while animals use scent to mark their territory, flowers emit scent as an attracting means for reproduction. g_text_translate.de:
Diese findet man nicht nur bei Tieren, sondern auch in der Blumenwelt. Der einzige Unterschied besteht darin, dass Tiere Düfte verwenden, um ihr Territorium zu markieren, während Blumen Düfte als anziehendes Mittel zur Fortpflanzung abgeben. Eine Blume, die sich nicht bewegen kann, kann sich nur mit Hilfe von Bienen, Schmetterlingen und Insekten vermehren. Daher gibt jede Blume einen einzigartigen Geruch ab, um andere Kreaturen zur Fortpflanzung anzulocken. Zum Beispiel riecht eine Blume, die Bienen oder Schmetterlinge anlocken muss, süß, und eine Blume, die Insekten anlocken möchte, riecht nach Obst oder Schimmel. Es soll das zu verführende Objekt mit einem angenehmen Duft anlocken. Abhängig von den Gewohnheiten des anzulockenden Ziels ist auch die Methode zum Ausströmen des Geruchs unterschiedlich. Nachtaktive Motten können nicht mit visuellen Informationen lokalisiert werden. Blumen, die Motten anlocken sollen, verströmen also einen Duft, der sich weiter ausbreitet als andere Blumen.

g_text_translate.it:
Questo può essere trovato non solo negli animali ma anche nel mondo dei fiori. Pertanto, ogni fiore emette un odore unico come strumento per attirare altre creature per aiutarle a riprodursi. Ad esempio, un fiore che deve attirare api o farfalle ha un profumo dolce e un fiore che vuole attirare insetti odora di frutta o muffa. Le falene attive di notte non possono essere localizzate utilizzando informazioni visive. Quindi, i fiori che dovrebbero attirare le falene emettono un profumo che viaggia più lontano di altri fiori.

g_text_translate.ru:
Это можно найти не только у животных, но и в мире цветов. Единственное отличие состоит в том, что животные используют запах, чтобы отметить свою территорию, а цветы испускают запах как средство привлечения для размножения. Цветок, который не может двигаться, может размножаться только с помощью пчел, бабочек и насекомых. Таким образом, каждый цветок излучает уникальный запах и служит инструментом привлечения других существ для размножения. Например, цветок, которому нужно привлечь пчел или бабочек, пахнет сладко, а цветок, который хочет привлечь насекомых, пахнет фруктами или плесенью. Он предназначен для того, чтобы соблазнить объект соблазнения, испуская приятный аромат. Кроме того, в зависимости от привычек привлекаемой цели различается и способ истощения запаха. Моль, активную в ночное время, невозможно обнаружить с помощью визуальной информации. Итак, цветы, которые должны привлекать моль, испускают аромат, который распространяется дальше, чем другие цветы.

g_text_translate.pt:
Isto pode ser encontrado não apenas nos animais, mas também no mundo das flores. A única diferença é que enquanto os animais usam o cheiro para marcar seu território, as flores emitem perfume como meio de atração para a reprodução. Uma flor que não se move só pode se reproduzir com a ajuda de abelhas, borboletas e insetos. Portanto, cada flor emite um odor único como ferramenta para atrair outras criaturas para ajudar na reprodução. Por exemplo, uma flor que precisa atrair abelhas ou borboletas tem um cheiro doce, e uma flor que quer atrair insetos tem um cheiro de fruta ou mofo. Tem como objetivo atrair o objeto a ser seduzido, exalando um perfume agradável. Além disso, dependendo dos hábitos do alvo a ser atraído, o método de exalar o cheiro é diferente. As mariposas ativas à noite não podem ser localizadas por meio de informações visuais. Assim, as flores que supostamente atraem as mariposas emitem um perfume que viaja mais longe do que outras flores.

g_text_translate.hi:
यह न केवल जानवरों में बल्कि फूलों की दुनिया में भी पाया जा सकता है। अंतर केवल इतना है कि जहां जानवर अपने क्षेत्र को चिह्नित करने के लिए गंध का उपयोग करते हैं, वहीं फूल प्रजनन के लिए एक आकर्षक साधन के रूप में गंध का उत्सर्जन करते हैं। एक फूल जो हिल नहीं सकता वह केवल मधुमक्खियों, तितलियों और कीड़ों की मदद से ही प्रजनन कर सकता है। इसलिए, प्रत्येक फूल अन्य प्राणियों को प्रजनन में मदद करने के लिए आकर्षित करने के साधन के रूप में एक अनोखी गंध उत्सर्जित करता है। उदाहरण के लिए, जिस फूल को मधुमक्खियों या तितलियों को आकर्षित करने की आवश्यकता होती है, उसकी गंध मीठी होती है, और जो फूल कीड़ों को आकर्षित करना चाहता है उसकी गंध फल या फफूंद जैसी होती है। इसका उद्देश्य मनभावन सुगंध देकर वस्तु को लुभाना है। साथ ही, जिस लक्ष्य को आकर्षित किया जाना है उसकी आदतों के आधार पर गंध निकालने का तरीका भी अलग-अलग होता है। रात में सक्रिय रहने वाले पतंगों को दृश्य जानकारी का उपयोग करके नहीं खोजा जा सकता है। तो, जिन फूलों से पतंगों को आकर्षित करने की अपेक्षा की जाती है, वे ऐसी गंध छोड़ते हैं जो अन्य फूलों की तुलना में दूर तक जाती है।

g_text_translate.tr:
Bu sadece hayvanlarda değil çiçeklerin dünyasında da mevcut. Tek fark, hayvanlar kendi bölgelerini işaretlemek için koku kullanırken, çiçekler üreme için çekici bir araç olarak koku yaymaktadır. Hareket edemeyen bir çiçek ancak arıların, kelebeklerin ve böceklerin yardımıyla çoğalabilir. Bu nedenle her çiçek, üremeye yardımcı olmak amacıyla diğer canlıları cezbetmek için bir araç olarak kendine özgü bir koku yayar. Örneğin, arıları veya kelebekleri çekmesi gereken bir çiçek tatlı kokar, böcekleri çekmesi gereken bir çiçek ise meyve veya küf gibi kokar. Hoş bir koku yayarak baştan çıkarılacak nesneyi cezbetmek amaçlanır. Ayrıca çekilecek hedefin alışkanlıklarına bağlı olarak kokuyu yayma yöntemi de farklıdır. Geceleri aktif olan güvelerin yeri görsel bilgilerle belirlenemez. Yani güveleri çekmesi gereken çiçekler, diğer çiçeklerden daha uzağa yayılan bir koku yayarlar.

explain.vi:
Nội dung phía trước của đoạn văn này là gì?
1. Tập tính sử dụng mùi làm phương tiện của động vật
2. Tâm lý thể hiện cảm xúc thông qua mùi hương của động vật
3. Đặc tính vừa ngửi vừa tìm kiếm thức ăn của động vật 
4. Khả năng dự đoán sự thay đổi tự nhiên bằng cách sử dụng mùi hương của động vật 
--------------------
Câu đầu tiên cho biết "điều này" không chỉ có ở động vật mà còn có ở các loài hoa. Sau đó đoạn văn đề cập đến tập tính sử dụng mùi làm phương tiện sinh sản của hoa. Như vậy "điều này" chính là tập tính sử dụng mùi làm phương tiện. 1 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
What is the content before this paragraph?
1. Animal's habit of using smell as a means
2. Psychological expression of emotions through the scent of animals
3. The animal's ability to smell and forage at the same time
4. The ability to predict natural change using an animal's scent
--------------------
The first sentence says "this" is not only found in animals but also in flowers. The paragraph then mentions the habit of using smell as a means of reproduction in flowers. So "this" is the habit of using smell as a means. 1 is the correct answer.
explain.fr:
Quel est le contenu avant ce paragraphe ?
1. L'habitude de l'animal d'utiliser l'odorat comme moyen
2. Expression psychologique des émotions à travers l'odeur des animaux
3. La capacité de l'animal à sentir et à se nourrir en même temps
4. La capacité de prédire les changements naturels en utilisant l'odeur d'un animal
--------------------
La première phrase dit "cela" ne se trouve pas seulement chez les animaux mais aussi dans les fleurs. Le paragraphe mentionne ensuite l'habitude d'utiliser l'odorat comme moyen de reproduction dans les fleurs. Donc "ceci" est l'habitude d'utiliser l'odorat comme moyen. 1 est la bonne réponse.

1. Kebiasaan hewan menggunakan bau sebagai sarana

general.g_text: 허리띠는 거처를 이리저리 옮기면서 살던 북방 유목 민족의 문화에서부터 시작되었다. 유목민들은 손을 자유롭게 움직이기 위해 자주 사용하는 물건들을 허리띠에 매달고 생활했다. 이후 사람들이 정착 생활을 하게 되면서 (        ). 그 대신 소원을 빌거나 자신의 재력을 과시하기 위해서 허리띠에 금이나 옥으로 만든 약통, 물고기, 칼 등의 장식을 달게 되었다. 이 장식들은 각각 상징하는 것이 다른데 약통은 병의 치료를, 물고기는 풍부한 음식을 기원하는 의미를 나타냈다. 특히 칼은 권력을 상징하는 것으로 주로 왕들의 허리띠 장식에 쓰였다. 또 사람들은 자신의 부유함을 과시하기 위해서 허리띠를 남들보다 크게 만들기도 했으며 화려하게 보이기 위해 여러 줄을 엮어 길게 늘어뜨리기도 했다. 이와 같이 허리띠는 생활의 편의를 위한 것에서 상징적 의미를 나타내는 것으로 그 목적이 달라져 갔다.

g_text_translate.vi: Thắt lưng bắt nguồn từ văn hóa của dân tộc du mục phương Bắc sống di chuyển chỗ ở khắp nơi. Những người du mục treo trên thắt lưng những đồ vật thường xuyên sử dụng để di chuyển tay một cách tự do. Sau đó, (        ) khi mọi người sống định cư. Thay vào đó, để cầu nguyện hoặc phô trương sức mạnh tài chính của bản thân, người ta đã đeo những đồ trang trí như hộp thuốc, cá, dao,...  làm từ vàng hoặc ngọc trên thắt lưng. Những đồ trang trí này tượng trưng cho nhiều thứ khác nhau, hộp thuốc thể hiện ý nghĩa điều trị bệnh tật, cá thể hiện mong muốn thức ăn dồi dào. Đặc biệt, dao là biểu tượng của quyền lực và chủ yếu được sử dụng để trang trí thắt lưng của các vị vua. Ngoài ra, để phô trương sự giàu có của bản thân, người ta đã làm cho thắt lưng to hơn so với những người khác, và để trông rực rỡ, họ bện nhiều sợi dây vào rồi xõa dài xuống. Thắt lưng đã bị thay đổi mục đích từ việc giúp cho cuộc sống được tiện lợi sang thể hiện những ý nghĩa mang tính tượng trưng.
g_text_translate.en: The belt began with the culture of the northern nomadic peoples who lived from place to place. Nomads used to hang frequently used items on their belts to move their hands freely. After that, as people began to settle down, the original meaning of the belt of practicality disappeared. Instead, to make a wish or to show off one's wealth, gold or jade medicine boxes, fish, and swords were attached to the belt. Each of these decorations symbolizes different things, with a medicine box representing a cure for illness and a fish representing a wish for abundant food. In particular, the sword was a symbol of power and was mainly used to decorate the belts of kings. In addition, to show off their wealth, people made their belts larger than others, and to show off their splendor, they woven several lines to make them look glamorous. In this way, the purpose of the belt has changed from being for the convenience of life to representing a symbolic meaning.
g_text_translate.fr:
La ceinture a commencé avec la culture des peuples nomades du nord qui vivaient d'un endroit à l'autre. Les nomades avaient l'habitude d'accrocher les objets fréquemment utilisés à leur ceinture pour bouger librement leurs mains. Après cela, alors que les gens commençaient à s'installer, la signification originale de la ceinture de praticité a disparu. Au lieu de cela, pour faire un vœu ou pour montrer sa richesse, des boîtes de médecine en or ou en jade, des poissons et des épées étaient attachés à la ceinture. Chacune de ces décorations symbolise différentes choses, avec une boîte à médicaments représentant un remède contre la maladie et un poisson représentant un souhait de nourriture abondante. En particulier, l'épée était un symbole de pouvoir et était principalement utilisée pour orner les ceintures des rois. De plus, pour montrer leur richesse, les gens ont fait leurs ceintures plus grandes que les autres, et pour montrer leur splendeur, ils ont tissé plusieurs lignes pour les rendre glamour. De cette façon, le but de la ceinture est passé de la commodité de la vie à la représentation d'une signification symbolique.

#######

The belt began with the culture of the northern nomadic peoples who lived from place to place. Nomads used to hang frequently used items on their belts to move their hands freely. After that, as people began to settle down, the original meaning of the belt of practicality disappeared. Instead, to make a wish or to show off one's wealth, gold or jade medicine boxes, fish, and swords were attached to the belt. Each of these decorations symbolizes different things, with a medicine box representing a cure for illness and a fish representing a wish for abundant food. In particular, the sword was a symbol of power and was mainly used to decorate the belts of kings. In addition, to show off their wealth, people made their belts larger than others, and to show off their splendor, they woven several lines to make them look glamorous. In this way, the purpose of the belt has changed from being for the convenience of life to representing a symbolic meaning.
g_text_translate.es:
El cinturón comenzó con la cultura de los pueblos nómadas del norte que vivían de un lugar a otro. Los nómadas solían colgar artículos de uso frecuente en sus cinturones para mover sus manos libremente. Después de eso, cuando la gente comenzó a establecerse, el significado original del cinturón de practicidad desapareció. En cambio, para pedir un deseo o mostrar la riqueza de uno, se adjuntaban al cinturón cajas de medicinas de oro o jade, peces y espadas. Cada una de estas decoraciones simboliza cosas diferentes, con un botiquín que representa una cura para una enfermedad y un pez que representa un deseo de comida abundante. En particular, la espada era un símbolo de poder y se usaba principalmente para decorar los cinturones de los reyes. Además, para mostrar su riqueza, las personas hacían sus cinturones más grandes que los demás, y para mostrar su esplendor, tejían varias líneas para que se vieran glamorosos. De esta forma, el propósito del cinturón ha pasado de ser para la comodidad de la vida a representar un significado simbólico.

#######

The belt began with the culture of the northern nomadic peoples who lived from place to place. Nomads used to hang frequently used items on their belts to move their hands freely. After that, as people began to settle down, the original meaning of the belt of practicality disappeared. Instead, to make a wish or to show off one's wealth, gold or jade medicine boxes, fish, and swords were attached to the belt. Each of these decorations symbolizes different things, with a medicine box representing a cure for illness and a fish representing a wish for abundant food. In particular, the sword was a symbol of power and was mainly used to decorate the belts of kings. In addition, to show off their wealth, people made their belts larger than others, and to show off their splendor, they woven several lines to make them look glamorous. In this way, the purpose of the belt has changed from being for the convenience of life to representing a symbolic meaning.
g_text_translate.id:
Sabuk dimulai dengan budaya masyarakat nomaden utara yang hidup dari satu tempat ke tempat lain. Pengembara biasa menggantung barang-barang yang sering digunakan di ikat pinggang mereka untuk menggerakkan tangan mereka dengan bebas. Setelah itu, ketika orang-orang mulai tenang, makna asli dari sabuk kepraktisan menghilang. Sebagai gantinya, untuk membuat keinginan atau untuk memamerkan kekayaan seseorang, kotak obat emas atau batu giok, ikan, dan pedang diikatkan ke ikat pinggang. Masing-masing dekorasi melambangkan hal yang berbeda, dengan kotak obat yang mewakili obat untuk penyakit dan ikan yang mewakili keinginan untuk makanan yang berlimpah. Secara khusus, pedang adalah simbol kekuatan dan terutama digunakan untuk menghias ikat pinggang raja. Selain itu, untuk memamerkan kekayaan mereka, orang membuat ikat pinggang mereka lebih besar dari yang lain, dan untuk memamerkan kemegahan mereka, mereka membuat beberapa garis agar terlihat glamor. Dengan cara ini, tujuan sabuk telah berubah dari untuk kenyamanan hidup menjadi mewakili makna simbolis.

#######

The belt began with the culture of the northern nomadic peoples who lived from place to place. Nomads used to hang frequently used items on their belts to move their hands freely. After that, as people began to settle down, the original meaning of the belt of practicality disappeared. Instead, to make a wish or to show off one's wealth, gold or jade medicine boxes, fish, and swords were attached to the belt. Each of these decorations symbolizes different things, with a medicine box representing a cure for illness and a fish representing a wish for abundant food. In particular, the sword was a symbol of power and was mainly used to decorate the belts of kings. In addition, to show off their wealth, people made their belts larger than others, and to show off their splendor, they woven several lines to make them look glamorous. In this way, the purpose of the belt has changed from being for the convenience of life to representing a symbolic meaning.
g_text_translate.ja:
ベルトは、あちこちに住んでいた北部の遊牧民の文化から始まりました。遊牧民はよく使うものをベルトにぶら下げて手を自由に動かしていました。その後、人々が定着するにつれ、実用性の帯の本来の意味は消えていきました。代わりに、願い事をしたり、自分の富を誇示したりするために、金または翡翠の薬箱、魚、剣がベルトに取り付けられました.薬箱は病気を治す、魚は豊穣を願うなど、それぞれの飾りが象徴しています。特に剣は権力の象徴であり、主に王の帯を飾るために使用されました。また、人々は富を誇示するために帯を他のものより大きくし、華麗さを誇示するために数本の線を編んで華やかに見せました。このように、帯の目的は生活の利便性から象徴的な意味へと変化しました。

#######

g_text_translate.de:
Der Gürtel begann mit der Kultur der nördlichen Nomadenvölker, die von Ort zu Ort lebten. Früher hängten Nomaden häufig benutzte Gegenstände an ihren Gürtel, um ihre Hände frei bewegen zu können. Danach, als die Menschen begannen, sich niederzulassen, verschwand die ursprüngliche Bedeutung des praktischen Gürtels. Um sich etwas zu wünschen oder seinen Reichtum zu zeigen, wurden stattdessen Gold- oder Jade-Arzneischachteln, Fische und Schwerter am Gürtel befestigt. Jede dieser Dekorationen symbolisiert verschiedene Dinge, wobei eine Medizinbox ein Heilmittel gegen Krankheiten darstellt und ein Fisch den Wunsch nach reichlich Nahrung darstellt. Insbesondere das Schwert war ein Symbol der Macht und wurde hauptsächlich verwendet, um die Gürtel der Könige zu schmücken. Um ihren Reichtum zu zeigen, machten die Menschen ihre Gürtel größer als andere, und um ihre Pracht zu zeigen, flochten sie mehrere Linien, um sie glamourös aussehen zu lassen. Auf diese Weise hat sich der Zweck des Gürtels von der Bequemlichkeit des Lebens zu einer symbolischen Bedeutung gewandelt.

#######

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
这条带始于从一个地方到另一个地方生活的北方游牧民族的文化。游牧民族习惯将经常使用的物品挂在腰带上，以自由移动双手。之后，随着人们开始安定下来，实用腰带的本义就消失了。取而代之的是，为了许愿或炫富，金或玉的药盒、鱼和剑都系在腰带上。这些装饰品中的每一个都象征着不同的事物，一个代表治愈疾病的药盒和一条代表对丰富食物的愿望的鱼。尤其是剑是权力的象征，主要用于装饰国王的腰带。此外，为了炫富，人们把腰带比别人的大，为了炫耀自己的光彩，他们编织了几条线，使它们看起来光彩照人。就这样，腰带的用途从为了生活的方便变成了代表一种象征意义。

g_text_translate.it:
La cintura è iniziata con la cultura dei popoli nomadi del nord che vivevano da un posto all'altro. I nomadi erano soliti appendere alla cintura oggetti di uso frequente per muovere liberamente le mani. Successivamente, quando le persone hanno cominciato a sistemarsi, il significato originale della cintura della praticità è scomparso. Invece, per esprimere un desiderio o per mostrare la propria ricchezza, alla cintura venivano attaccate scatole di medicinali d'oro o di giada, pesci e spade. Ognuna di queste decorazioni simboleggia cose diverse, con una scatola di medicinali che rappresenta una cura per la malattia e un pesce che rappresenta un desiderio di cibo abbondante. In particolare, la spada era simbolo di potere e veniva usata principalmente per decorare le cinture dei re. Inoltre, per mostrare la loro ricchezza, le persone hanno fatto le loro cinture più grandi delle altre e per mostrare il loro splendore, hanno tessuto diverse linee per farle sembrare glamour. In questo modo, lo scopo della cintura è cambiato dall'essere per la comodità della vita a rappresentare un significato simbolico.

g_text_translate.ru:
Пояс начался с культуры северных кочевых народов, живших с места на место. Часто используемые предметы кочевники вешали на пояс, чтобы руки могли свободно двигать. После этого, когда люди стали обживаться, первоначальное значение пояса практичности исчезло. Вместо этого, чтобы загадать желание или продемонстрировать свое богатство, к поясу прикрепляли золотые или нефритовые аптечки, рыбу и мечи. Каждое из этих украшений символизирует разные вещи: аптечка — лекарство от болезней, а рыба — желание обильной еды. В частности, меч был символом власти и в основном использовался для украшения поясов королей. Кроме того, чтобы продемонстрировать свое богатство, люди делали свои пояса крупнее других, а чтобы продемонстрировать свое великолепие, плели несколько нитей, чтобы они выглядели гламурно. Таким образом, цель ремня изменилась: от удобства жизни он стал символическим.

g_text_translate.th:
เข็มขัดเริ่มต้นด้วยวัฒนธรรมของชนเผ่าเร่ร่อนทางตอนเหนือที่อาศัยอยู่จากที่หนึ่งไปยังอีกที่หนึ่ง คนเร่ร่อนเคยแขวนสิ่งของที่ใช้บ่อยไว้บนเข็มขัดเพื่อขยับมือได้อย่างอิสระ หลังจากนั้นเมื่อผู้คนเริ่มปักหลัก ความหมายดั้งเดิมของเข็มขัดแห่งการปฏิบัติจริงก็หายไป ในทางกลับกัน เพื่อขอพรหรืออวดความมั่งคั่ง กลับมีกล่องยาทองคำหรือหยก ปลา และดาบติดอยู่กับเข็มขัด การตกแต่งแต่ละอย่างเหล่านี้เป็นสัญลักษณ์ของสิ่งต่าง ๆ ที่แตกต่างกัน โดยมีกล่องยาที่แสดงถึงการรักษาโรค และปลาที่เป็นตัวแทนของความปรารถนาที่จะมีอาหารที่อุดมสมบูรณ์ โดยเฉพาะอย่างยิ่งดาบเป็นสัญลักษณ์ของพลังและส่วนใหญ่ใช้เพื่อประดับเข็มขัดของกษัตริย์ นอกจากนี้ เพื่ออวดความมั่งคั่ง ผู้คนจึงทำเข็มขัดให้ใหญ่กว่าคนอื่นๆ และเพื่ออวดความยิ่งใหญ่ พวกเขาทอเส้นหลายเส้นเพื่อให้ดูหรูหรา ด้วยเหตุนี้ จุดประสงค์ของเข็มขัดจึงเปลี่ยนจากการมีไว้เพื่อความสะดวกสบายของชีวิตมาเป็นการแสดงความหมายเชิงสัญลักษณ์

g_text_translate.pt:
O cinturão começou com a cultura dos povos nômades do norte que viviam de um lugar para outro. Os nômades costumavam pendurar itens usados ​​com frequência em seus cintos para mover as mãos livremente. Depois disso, à medida que as pessoas começaram a se estabelecer, o significado original do cinturão da praticidade desapareceu. Em vez disso, para fazer um desejo ou exibir sua riqueza, caixas de remédios de ouro ou jade, peixes e espadas eram presas ao cinto. Cada uma dessas decorações simboliza coisas diferentes, com uma caixa de remédios representando a cura para doenças e um peixe representando o desejo de comida abundante. Em particular, a espada era um símbolo de poder e era usada principalmente para decorar os cintos dos reis. Além disso, para exibir sua riqueza, as pessoas faziam seus cintos maiores que os outros e, para exibir seu esplendor, teciam várias linhas para torná-los glamorosos. Dessa forma, a finalidade do cinto deixou de ser uma comodidade de vida para representar um significado simbólico.

g_text_translate.hi:
बेल्ट की शुरुआत उत्तरी खानाबदोश लोगों की संस्कृति से हुई जो एक स्थान से दूसरे स्थान पर रहते थे। खानाबदोश अपने हाथों को स्वतंत्र रूप से चलाने के लिए अक्सर इस्तेमाल की जाने वाली वस्तुओं को अपनी बेल्ट पर लटकाते थे। उसके बाद, जैसे-जैसे लोग बसने लगे, व्यावहारिकता की बेल्ट का मूल अर्थ गायब हो गया। इसके बजाय, इच्छा करने या किसी के धन को दिखाने के लिए, सोने या जेड दवा के बक्से, मछली और तलवारें बेल्ट से जुड़ी हुई थीं। इनमें से प्रत्येक सजावट अलग-अलग चीजों का प्रतीक है, जिसमें एक दवा का डिब्बा बीमारी के इलाज का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है और एक मछली प्रचुर भोजन की इच्छा का प्रतिनिधित्व करती है। विशेष रूप से, तलवार शक्ति का प्रतीक थी और मुख्य रूप से राजाओं की बेल्ट को सजाने के लिए उपयोग की जाती थी। इसके अलावा, लोगों ने अपनी संपत्ति का प्रदर्शन करने के लिए अपनी बेल्ट को दूसरों की तुलना में बड़ा बनाया और अपनी भव्यता दिखाने के लिए, उन्होंने उन्हें ग्लैमरस दिखाने के लिए कई लाइनें बुनीं। इस तरह, बेल्ट का उद्देश्य जीवन की सुविधा के बजाय एक प्रतीकात्मक अर्थ का प्रतिनिधित्व करने के लिए बदल गया है।

g_text_translate.tr:
Kemer, bir yerden bir yere yaşayan kuzeydeki göçebe halkların kültürüyle başladı. Göçebeler ellerini özgürce hareket ettirmek için sık kullandıkları eşyaları kemerlerine asarlardı. Bundan sonra insanlar yerleşmeye başladıkça pratiklik kemerinin orijinal anlamı ortadan kalktı. Bunun yerine, bir dilek tutmak veya zenginliği göstermek için kemere altın veya yeşimden ilaç kutuları, balık ve kılıçlar takılırdı. Bu süslemelerin her biri farklı şeyleri simgeliyor; hastalıklara şifayı temsil eden bir ilaç kutusu ve bol yiyecek arzusunu temsil eden bir balık. Özellikle kılıç bir güç simgesiydi ve çoğunlukla kralların kemerlerini süslemek için kullanılıyordu. Ayrıca insanlar zenginliklerini göstermek için kemerlerini diğerlerinden daha büyük yaparlar ve ihtişamlarını göstermek için birkaç çizgi örerek göz alıcı görünmelerini sağlarlardı. Böylelikle kemerin amacı, yaşama kolaylık sağlamaktan, sembolik bir anlamı temsil etmeye dönüşmüştür.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp nhất để làm tựa đề cho đoạn văn.
1. Ý nghĩa tượng trưng của thắt lưng 
2. Hiệu quả trang trí của thắt lưng 
3. Nguyên liệu và việc chế tác thắt lưng 
4. Nguồn gốc và sự biến đổi của thắt lưng
--------------------
Đoạn văn cho biết nguồn gốc của thắt lưng là từ văn hóa của dân tộc du mục, cũng như sự biến đổi về ý nghĩa của thắt lưng. 4 là đáp án đúng.

content.q_text: 이 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 여러 줄로 길게 만든 허리띠는 왕의 권력을 의미했다.
허리띠에 매다는 장식들은 금과 옥을 재료로 만들었다.
재력을 과시하려는 사람들은 물고기 장식을 허리띠에 달았다.
사람들은 허리띠를 크고 화려하게 만들어 부유함을 기원하였다.

explain.vi:
Chọn câu giống với nội dung của đoạn văn.
1. Một chiếc thắt lưng được làm dài từ nhiều sợi biểu tượng cho quyền lực của nhà vua.
2. Các đồ trang trí buộc trên thắt lưng được làm bằng vàng và ngọc bích.
3. Những người muốn phô trương sự giàu có của mình thì đeo đồ trang trí hình cá vào thắt lưng của họ.
4. Người ta làm những chiếc thắt lưng lớn và hoa lệ để cầu giàu có.
--------------------
Người ta đã đeo những đồ trang trí như hộp thuốc, cá, dao,... trên thắt lưng. Như vậy 2 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the one that is similar to the content of the paragraph.
1. The long belt made of several lines symbolized the power of the king.
2. The decorations on the belt were made of gold and jade.
3. Those who want to show off their wealth put fish decorations on their belts.
4. People made large and luxurious belts to pray for wealth.
--------------------
People hang ornaments such as gold and jade medicine boxes, fish, knives,... on their belts. So 2 is the correct answer.
explain.fr:
Choisissez celui qui est similaire au contenu du paragraphe.
1. La longue ceinture composée de plusieurs lignes symbolisait le pouvoir du roi.
2. Les décorations de la ceinture étaient en or et en jade.
3. Ceux qui veulent montrer leur richesse mettent des décorations de poisson à leur ceinture.
4. Les gens fabriquaient de grandes et luxueuses ceintures pour prier pour la richesse.
--------------------
Les gens accrochent des ornements tels que des boîtes de médicaments en or et en jade, des poissons, des couteaux,... à leur ceinture. Donc 2 est la bonne réponse.

1. The long belt made of several lines symbolized the power of the king.
2. The decorations on the belt were made of gold and jade.
3. Those who want to show off their wealth put fish decorations on their belts.
4. People made large and luxurious belts to pray for wealth.
--------------------
People hang ornaments such as gold and jade medicine boxes, fish, knives,... on their belts. So 2 is the correct answer.

वह चुनें जो पैराग्राफ की सामग्री के समान हो।

3. जो लोग अपने धन का प्रदर्शन करना चाहते हैं वे अपनी बेल्ट पर मछली की सजावट करते हैं।
4. लोगों ने धन की प्रार्थना के लिए बड़ी और शानदार बेल्टें बनाईं।
------------------------
लोग अपनी बेल्ट पर सोने और जेड दवा के बक्से, मछली, चाकू, जैसे आभूषण लटकाते हैं। अतः 2 सही उत्तर है।

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án đúng để điền vào phần (        ).
1. Chiếc thắt lưng đã được chế tác theo cách có hệ thống hơn
2. Nó được làm ra bằng cách bện nhiều dây để làm cho nó chắc chắn
3. Người ta bắt đầu nghiên cứu cách thuận tiện hơn để treo đồ
4. Ý nghĩa ban đầu của thắt lưng mang tính thực tiễn đã biến mất
--------------------
Dựa vào những ví dụ cụ thể, có thể thấy khi mọi người sống định cư, thắt lưng đã mất đi ý nghĩa ban đầu là mang tính thực tiễn, chuyển sang ý nghĩa mang tính tượng trưng. Như vậy 4 là đáp án đúng.

Choisissez la bonne réponse à remplir ( ).
1. Les ceintures ont été fabriquées de manière plus systématique
2. Plusieurs cordes ont été tissées ensemble pour le rendre plus solide
3. Les gens ont commencé à étudier comment accrocher les choses plus facilement
4. Le sens originel de la ceinture de praticité a disparu
--------------------
Sur la base d'exemples spécifiques, on peut voir que lorsque les gens vivent dans des colonies, les ceintures ont perdu leur sens original d'être pratique et ont changé pour un sens symbolique. Donc 4 est la bonne réponse.
